sdegeo:create-circle.description: Creates a 2D circular region. When optional start-angle and end-angle are specified, a circular arc is generated.

parameters.name: center-pos
parameters.desc: Center position of the circle, specified as `(position x y z)`. Defines the center of the circular region.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.desc: Radius of the circle (REAL). Defines the distance from the center to the boundary.

parameters.name: region-material
parameters.desc: DATEX material name assigned to the region, e.g. `"Silicon"`

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Region name string assigned to the created region, e.g. `"R.Substrate"`

parameters.name: start-angle
parameters.desc: Optional start angle in degrees. When specified together with end-angle, a circular arc is generated instead of a full circle.

parameters.name: end-angle
parameters.desc: Optional end angle in degrees. When specified together with start-angle, a circular arc is generated instead of a full circle.

sdegeo:create-cone.description: Creates a circular cone (or elliptical cone if ratio and position3 are specified). Supports both `(position ...)` and bare xyz syntax for axis endpoints.

parameters.name: start-axis
parameters.desc: Base position of the cone (POSITION). Specifies the start axis position or base of the cone. Use `(position x y z)` or bare `x y z`.

parameters.name: end-axis
parameters.desc: Top position of the cone (POSITION). Specifies the end axis position or top of the cone. Use `(position x y z)` or bare `x y z`.

parameters.name: base-radius
parameters.desc: Radius at start-axis (REAL, must be greater than zero)

parameters.name: top-radius
parameters.desc: Radius at end-axis (REAL, can be zero)

parameters.name: ratio
parameters.desc: Optional ratio between the major and minor axes of the ellipse (REAL). When specified, an elliptical cone is created instead of a circular cone.

parameters.name: position3
parameters.desc: Optional point (POSITION) defining the major axis direction for elliptical cone. The vector from the projection of this point onto the cone axis to the point itself specifies the major axis. If not specified, the major axis is defined by the projection of the x-axis of the active coordinate system. Cannot lie on the axis of the cone, otherwise an error occurs.

parameters.name: region-material
parameters.desc: DATEX material name assigned to the body, e.g. `"Gold"`

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Region name string assigned to the body

sdegeo:create-cuboid.description: Creates a solid block (3D cuboid) defined by two diagonal corner positions.

parameters.name: diagonal1
parameters.desc: First diagonal corner (POSITION) `(position x y z)`. Specifies one corner of the block.

parameters.name: diagonal2
parameters.desc: Second diagonal corner (POSITION) `(position x y z)`. Specifies the opposite corner of the block. The block is oriented with respect to the current work plane.

parameters.name: region-material
parameters.desc: DATEX material name assigned to the body, e.g. `"Silicon"`

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Region name string assigned to the body

sdegeo:create-cylinder.description: Creates a cylinder (or elliptical cylinder if ratio and position3 are specified). Supports both `(position ...)` and bare xyz syntax for endpoints.

parameters.name: start-pos
parameters.desc: Start position of the cylinder axis (POSITION). Specifies the start position of the cylinder. Use `(position x y z)` or bare `x y z`.

parameters.name: end-pos
parameters.desc: End position of the cylinder axis (POSITION). Specifies the end position of the cylinder. Use `(position x y z)` or bare `x y z`.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.desc: Radius of the cylinder (REAL). Specifies the radii for both the base and top of the cylinder.

parameters.name: ratio
parameters.desc: Optional ratio between the major and minor axes of the ellipse (REAL). When specified, an elliptical cylinder is created.

parameters.name: position3
parameters.desc: Optional point (POSITION) defining the major axis direction for elliptical cylinder. The vector from the projection of this point onto the cylinder axis to the point itself specifies the major axis. If not specified, the major axis is defined by the projection of the x-axis of the active coordinate system onto the plane defined by position1 and the vector from position1 to position2. Cannot lie on the axis of the cylinder, otherwise an error occurs.

parameters.name: region-material
parameters.desc: DATEX material name assigned to the body, e.g. `"Gold"`

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Region name string assigned to the body

sdegeo:create-ellipse.description: Creates a 2D elliptical region. position1 is the center, position2 is the endpoint of the major axis, ratio is the major/minor axis ratio.

parameters.name: position1
parameters.desc: Center position of the ellipse (POSITION) `(position x y z)`. The ellipse is generated on the active work plane using the active coordinate system.

parameters.name: position2
parameters.desc: Endpoint of the major axis (POSITION). Together with position1, defines the direction and length of the major axis.

parameters.name: ratio
parameters.desc: Ratio of the major and minor axes (REAL). Can be greater than 1, in which case the minor axis becomes the major axis.

parameters.name: region-material
parameters.desc: DATEX material name assigned to the region, e.g. `"PolySi"`

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Region name string assigned to the region

sdegeo:create-ellipsoid.description: Creates a 3D ellipsoid. position1 is the center, position2 is the endpoint of the major axis, ratio is the major/minor axis ratio.

parameters.name: position1
parameters.desc: Center position of the ellipsoid (POSITION) `(position x y z)`. The ellipsoid is generated on the active work plane using the active coordinate system.

parameters.name: position2
parameters.desc: Endpoint of the major axis (POSITION). Together with position1, defines the direction and length of the major axis.

parameters.name: ratio
parameters.desc: Ratio of the major and minor axes (REAL). Can be greater than 1, in which case the minor axis becomes the major axis.

parameters.name: region-material
parameters.desc: DATEX material name assigned to the body, e.g. `"PolySi"`

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Region name string assigned to the body

sdegeo:create-linear-edge.description: Creates a linear edge between two positions. Returns an EDGE entity.

parameters.name: position1
parameters.desc: Start (end) position of the linear edge (POSITION). Specifies one endpoint of the edge.

parameters.name: position2
parameters.desc: End position of the linear edge (POSITION). Specifies the other endpoint of the edge.

sdegeo:create-ot-ellipsoid.signature: (sdegeo:create-ot-ellipsoid center-position major-axis-position ratio truncate-angle-1 truncate-angle-2 region-material region-name)
sdegeo:create-ot-ellipsoid.description: Creates an oriented and truncated ellipsoid with flat top and bottom at specified cut-off angles.

parameters.name: center-position
parameters.desc: Center position of the ellipsoid (POSITION). Specifies the center position of the oriented and truncated ellipsoid.

parameters.name: major-axis-position
parameters.desc: Endpoint of the major axis of the untruncated ellipsoid (POSITION). Specifies the direction and length of the major axis.

parameters.name: ratio
parameters.desc: Ratio of the major and minor axes (REAL)

parameters.name: truncate-angle-1
parameters.desc: First cut-off angle (REAL, degrees). Determines where the ellipsoidal top is replaced by a flat top.

parameters.name: truncate-angle-2
parameters.desc: Second cut-off angle (REAL, degrees). Determines where the ellipsoidal bottom is replaced by a flat bottom.

parameters.name: region-material
parameters.desc: DATEX material name assigned to the body, e.g. `"Gold"`

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Region name string assigned to the body

sdegeo:create-ot-sphere.signature: (sdegeo:create-ot-sphere center-position orientation radius truncate-angle-1 truncate-angle-2 region-material region-name)
sdegeo:create-ot-sphere.description: Creates an oriented and truncated sphere with flat top and bottom. The orientation vector defines the pole direction.

parameters.name: center-position
parameters.desc: Center position of the sphere (POSITION). Specifies the center position of the oriented and truncated sphere.

parameters.name: orientation
parameters.desc: Orientation vector (GVECTOR) `(gvector xv yv vz)`. Specifies the normal vector of the truncated top and bottom. The sphere is oriented so that its poles are placed in this direction.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.desc: Radius of the sphere (REAL). Determines the size of the sphere.

parameters.name: truncate-angle-1
parameters.desc: First cut-off angle (REAL, degrees). Determines where the spherical top is replaced by a flat top.

parameters.name: truncate-angle-2
parameters.desc: Second cut-off angle (REAL, degrees). Determines where the spherical bottom is replaced by a flat bottom.

parameters.name: region-material
parameters.desc: DATEX material name assigned to the body, e.g. `"Silicon"`

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Region name string assigned to the body

sdegeo:create-polygon.description: Creates a 2D polygonal region defined by a list of vertex positions. The polygon is automatically closed if the first and last vertices differ.

parameters.name: vertex-list
parameters.desc: Position list (POSITION LIST) defining the vertices of the polygon, e.g. `(list (position 0 0 0) (position 1 0 0) ...)`. Can be open or closed; if the last vertex differs from the first, the first vertex closes the polygon.

parameters.name: region-material
parameters.desc: DATEX material name assigned to the region, e.g. `"Silicon"`

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Region name string assigned to the region

sdegeo:create-polyline-wire.description: Creates a polyline wire body consisting of linear edges only. Has no material or region attribute; useful for sweep operations.

parameters.name: vertex-list
parameters.desc: Position list (POSITION LIST) defining the vertex positions of the wire, e.g. `(list (position 0 0 0) (position 1 0 0) ...)`. The wire body has no attached material or region-name attribute and is not written to the output boundary file. Can be used during sweep operations with the sweep along a wire option.

sdegeo:create-prism.signature: (sdegeo:create-prism centerposition height major-radius minor-radius nsides region-material region-name)
sdegeo:create-prism.description: Creates a solid prism with nsides sides (>= 3). Centered about centerposition with height along z-axis.

parameters.name: centerposition
parameters.desc: Center position of the prism (POSITION). The prism is centered about this position.

parameters.name: height
parameters.desc: Height of the prism along the z-axis. If height is zero, the resulting body consists of only one polygonal-sided sheet face lying in the xy plane.

parameters.name: major-radius
parameters.desc: Radius of the prism along the x-axis (REAL)

parameters.name: minor-radius
parameters.desc: Radius of the prism along the y-axis (REAL)

parameters.name: nsides
parameters.desc: Number of sides of the prism (INTEGER, must be >= 3)

parameters.name: region-material
parameters.desc: DATEX material name assigned to the body, e.g. `"PolySilicon"`

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Region name string assigned to the body

sdegeo:create-pyramid.signature: (sdegeo:create-pyramid centerposition height major-radius minor-radius nsides top region-material region-name)
sdegeo:create-pyramid.description: Creates a solid pyramid with nsides sides (>= 3). The `top` parameter specifies the major axis length at the top.

parameters.name: centerposition
parameters.desc: Center position of the pyramid (POSITION). The pyramid is centered about this position (the manual states 'origin').

parameters.name: height
parameters.desc: Height of the pyramid along the z-axis (REAL). If height is zero, the resulting body consists of only one polygonal-sided sheet face lying in the xy plane.

parameters.name: major-radius
parameters.desc: Base radius of the pyramid along the x-axis (REAL)

parameters.name: minor-radius
parameters.desc: Base radius of the pyramid along the y-axis (REAL)

parameters.name: nsides
parameters.desc: Number of sides of the pyramid (INTEGER, must be >= 3)

parameters.name: top
parameters.desc: Major axis length at the top of the pyramid (REAL). Specifies the size of the top face.

parameters.name: region-material
parameters.desc: DATEX material name assigned to the body, e.g. `"PolySilicon"`

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Region name string assigned to the body

sdegeo:create-rectangle.description: Creates a rectangular region defined by two diagonal corner positions.

parameters.name: pos1
parameters.desc: First corner position (POSITION) `(position x y z)`. One of the two opposite corner vertices defining the rectangle.

parameters.name: pos2
parameters.desc: Diagonally opposite corner position (POSITION) `(position x y z)`. The other of the two opposite corner vertices defining the rectangle.

parameters.name: material
parameters.desc: DATEX material name assigned to the region, e.g. `"Silicon"`

parameters.name: region
parameters.desc: Region name string assigned to the region, e.g. `"R.Substrate"`

sdegeo:create-reg-polygon.description: Creates a 2D regular polygonal region defined by center, radius, and optional number of sides and rotation angle.

parameters.name: center
parameters.desc: Center of the regular polygon (POSITION). Specifies the center position of the polygon.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.desc: Radius of the circumscribed circle (REAL). Defines the circle on which the vertex points of the polygon lie.

parameters.name: nsides
parameters.desc: Optional number of sides (INTEGER). Specifies how many sides the regular polygon has.

parameters.name: angle
parameters.desc: Optional rotation angle in degrees (REAL). Counterclockwise from the x-axis, defines the 'rotation' of the regular polygon.

parameters.name: region-material
parameters.desc: DATEX material name assigned to the region, e.g. `"Silicon"`

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Region name string assigned to the region

sdegeo:create-ruled-region.description: Creates a 2D ruled region between two existing edges. The edges cannot intersect each other.

parameters.name: edge1
parameters.desc: First edge entity (EDGE). One of the two existing edges defining the ruled region. Edge orientation is not important, since the ruled region is always created to form a simply connected region.

parameters.name: edge2
parameters.desc: Second edge entity (EDGE). The other defining edge. The two selected edges cannot intersect each other.

parameters.name: region-material
parameters.desc: DATEX material name assigned to the region, e.g. `"Silicon"`

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Region name string assigned to the region

sdegeo:create-sphere.description: Creates a sphere centered at a specified position. Supports both `(position ...)` and bare xyz syntax.

parameters.name: center-position
parameters.desc: Center position of the sphere (POSITION). Specifies the center position. Use `(position x y z)` or bare `x y z`.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.desc: Radius of the sphere (REAL). Determines the size of the sphere.

parameters.name: region-material
parameters.desc: DATEX material name assigned to the body, e.g. `"Gold"`

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Region name string assigned to the body

sdegeo:create-spline-wire.description: Creates a spline wire body from a list of vertex positions. Has no material or region attribute; useful for sweep operations.

parameters.name: vertex-list
parameters.desc: Position list (POSITION LIST) defining the vertex positions of the spline wire, e.g. `(list (position 0 0 0) (position 1 1 0) ...)`. The wire body has no attached material or region-name attribute and is not written to the output boundary file. Can be used during sweep operations with the sweep along a wire option.

sdegeo:create-torus.signature: (sdegeo:create-torus {center-position | {cx cy cz}} major-radius minor-radius region-material region-name)
sdegeo:create-torus.description: Creates a solid torus in the xy plane. Shape depends on radius ratio: donut (major > minor), apple (major < minor), or lemon (major < 0).

parameters.name: center-position
parameters.desc: Center position of the torus (POSITION). Specifies the center location of the torus. Use `(position x y z)` or bare `x y z`. The torus is defined in the xy plane of the active coordinate system and oriented using the normal gvector of the active coordinate system.

parameters.name: major-radius
parameters.desc: Distance from the center to the spine curve (REAL). The spine curve is swept around a circle having the minor radius to define the torus. Determines shape: if > minor-radius, produces a donut; if positive but < minor-radius, produces an apple; if negative, produces a lemon.

parameters.name: minor-radius
parameters.desc: Radius of the circular cross-section (REAL). The cross-section is swept around the spine curve to define the torus.

parameters.name: region-material
parameters.desc: DATEX material name assigned to the body, e.g. `"Gold"`

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Region name string assigned to the body

sdegeo:create-triangle.description: Creates a 2D triangular region defined by three vertex positions.

parameters.name: v1
parameters.desc: First vertex of the triangle (POSITION) `(position x y z)`

parameters.name: v2
parameters.desc: Second vertex of the triangle (POSITION) `(position x y z)`

parameters.name: v3
parameters.desc: Third vertex of the triangle (POSITION) `(position x y z)`

parameters.name: region-material
parameters.desc: DATEX material name assigned to the region, e.g. `"Silicon"`

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Region name string assigned to the region

sdegeo:extrude.description: Extrudes 2D bodies into 3D in the positive z-direction. Use `"all"` to extrude the complete model.

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of 2D bodies to extrude. Instead of a body list, the keyword "all" can be used to extrude the complete model. Also applies to Ref/Eval windows. Argument type: BODY LIST

parameters.name: extrusion-depth
parameters.desc: Specifies the extrusion depth in the positive z-direction. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: extrude-option
parameters.desc: Optional. Filters the input body-list: "2D" (default) extrudes only sheet bodies; "3D" extrudes only faces of solid bodies; "Mixed" extrudes both 2D and 3D entities. Argument type: STRING (optional)

sdegeo:mirror-selected.description: Reflects entities using a transform:reflection-type transform object. Replaces the obsolete `sdegeo:reflect`.

parameters.name: entity-list
parameters.desc: List of entities to mirror

parameters.name: transform
parameters.desc: Reflection data specified as a transform:reflection-type transform object

parameters.name: keep-flag
parameters.desc: If #t, the parent elements are kept; if #f, they are deleted. When #t and mirrored body overlaps original, geometric bodies are merged

parameters.name: reverse-DRS-flag
parameters.desc: If #t, doping and Ref/Eval wire bodies such as doping baseline directions are reversed before mirroring. Default is #f

sdegeo:reflect.description: Reflects entities across a plane defined by a base position and direction vector. **Obsolete** — use `sdegeo:mirror-selected` instead.

parameters.name: entity-list
parameters.desc: Body, body list, or "all" to reflect

parameters.name: base-position
parameters.desc: Base position used for the reflection operation

parameters.name: base-vector
parameters.desc: Gvector specifying the direction of the reflection. Region names get .x/.y/.z/.tr suffix based on direction

parameters.name: keep-flag
parameters.desc: If #t, parent elements are kept; if #f, parent elements are deleted

sdegeo:revolve.description: Revolves entities about an axis defined by a base position and direction vector through the specified angle.

parameters.name: entity-list
parameters.desc: Body, body list, or "all" to revolve

parameters.name: base-position
parameters.desc: Base position about which entities are revolved

parameters.name: base-vector
parameters.desc: Gvector specifying the axis of the revolve operation

parameters.name: angle
parameters.desc: Revolve angle in degrees

sdegeo:rotate-selected.description: Rotates entities using a transform:rotation-type transform object. Supports optional repeat count and overlap control rules.

parameters.name: entity-list
parameters.desc: Body or body list to rotate

parameters.name: transform
parameters.desc: Rotation data specified as a transform:rotation-type transform object

parameters.name: keep-flag
parameters.desc: If #t, the original entities are kept. An optional repeat-number can also be specified

parameters.name: repeat-number
parameters.desc: Number of times to repeat the rotation operation (optional, only when keep-flag is #t)

parameters.name: overlap-rule
parameters.desc: Controls automatic Boolean overlap removal. Default is #t (overlaps removed automatically). Can be #f (disabled) or a string like "ABA", "BAB"

sdegeo:align-horizontal.description: Aligns the specified vertices to a horizontal line at the given y-coordinate. For 2D models only.

parameters.name: vertex-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of vertices to align to the horizontal line. Argument type: VERTEX LIST

parameters.name: align-position
parameters.desc: Specifies the position (y-coordinate) of the horizontal alignment line. Argument type: REAL

sdegeo:align-horizontal-aut.description: Aligns the specified vertices to a horizontal line at their average y-coordinate. For 2D models only.

parameters.name: vertex-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of vertices. The align position (y-coordinate) is calculated automatically as the average y-coordinate of the specified vertices. Argument type: VERTEX LIST

sdegeo:align-to-line.description: Aligns the specified vertices to a line defined by two points by projecting them onto it. For 2D models only.

parameters.name: vertex-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of vertices to align to the line. Argument type: VERTEX LIST

parameters.name: pos1
parameters.desc: Specifies the first point of the alignment line. The point must lie on the same work plane as the listed vertices. The vertices are projected onto the specified line. Argument type: POSITION

parameters.name: pos2
parameters.desc: Specifies the second point of the alignment line. The point must lie on the same work plane as the listed vertices. Argument type: POSITION

sdegeo:align-vertical.description: Aligns the specified vertices to a vertical line at the given x-coordinate. For 2D models only.

parameters.name: vertex-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of vertices to align to the vertical line. Argument type: VERTEX LIST

parameters.name: align-position
parameters.desc: Specifies the new position (x-coordinate) of the vertical alignment line. Argument type: REAL

sdegeo:align-vertical-aut.description: Aligns the specified vertices to a vertical line at their average x-coordinate. For 2D models only.

parameters.name: vertex-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of vertices. The align position (x-coordinate) is calculated automatically as the average x-coordinate of the specified vertices. Argument type: VERTEX LIST

sdegeo:bool-intersect.description: Intersects the first body with the rest. Result inherits material and region from the first body.

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of bodies. The first body is intersected with the rest of the bodies from the list. The resulting body inherits the DATEX material attribute from the first body. Applies to both 2D and 3D bodies. Argument type: BODY LIST

sdegeo:bool-subtract.description: Subtracts the first body from the rest in the list. Result inherits material and region from the first body.

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of bodies. The first body is subtracted by the rest of the bodies from the list. The resulting body inherits the DATEX material attribute of the first body. Applies to both 2D and 3D bodies. Argument type: BODY LIST

sdegeo:bool-unite.description: Performs a Boolean unite on the specified body list. Result inherits material and region from the first body.

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of bodies to unite. The newly united body inherits the DATEX material attribute of the first body. Applies to both 2D and 3D bodies. Argument type: BODY LIST

sdegeo:chamfer.description: Performs 3D edge or vertex chamfering with the specified chamfer distance.

parameters.name: edge | vertex-list
parameters.desc: Specifies an edge, or a list of edges or vertices to be chamfered. If a vertex list is specified, all edges connected to the specified vertices will be chamfered. The list must contain all tangent continuous edges; otherwise, the operation may fail. Argument type: EDGE LIST | VERTEX LIST

parameters.name: chamfer-dist
parameters.desc: Specifies the chamfering distance used for the operation. The distance must be selected so that the resulting model is physically (topologically) correct. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: adaptive-chamfering
parameters.desc: Optional. If set to #t, adaptive chamfering is performed: if the operation fails using the original chamfer-dist value, it is repeated with a sequence of operations using smaller chamfer distances until it succeeds. Argument type: BOOLEAN (optional)

sdegeo:chamfer-2d.description: Performs a 2D chamfering operation on specified vertex or vertices.

parameters.name: vertex(s)
parameters.desc: Specifies a vertex or a list of vertices to chamfer. When chamfering is performed on a vertex shared by neighboring regions, all vertices must be included in the list. Argument type: VERTEX | VERTEX LIST

parameters.name: chamfer-dist
parameters.desc: Specifies the chamfering distance. Must be selected so that the operation does not result in an invalid geometry. Argument type: REAL

sdegeo:check-overlap.description: Checks for overlaps between geometric bodies. Returns null if none, or a list of overlapping body pairs.

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of bodies to check for overlaps. Use (get-body-list) to check the entire model. The list should contain only geometric bodies; DRS entities (Ref/Eval windows) are ignored. Argument type: BODY LIST

parameters.name: use-bbox-check
parameters.desc: Optional. If #t (default), uses a fast bounding-box overlap check (may report false overlaps). If #f, performs a more reliable precise check. Argument type: BOOLEAN (default is #t)

sdegeo:chop-domain.description: Cuts the 3D device to cover only the specified polygonal base in the z-direction. Device bottom must be on the xy plane.

parameters.name: point-pairs
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of x, y point pairs defining the polygonal base. The device is cut to cover only this polygon. The device must be placed at the xy-plane, and the cut is performed in the z-direction. Argument type: REAL REAL (a list of alternating x and y values)

sdegeo:delete-collinear-edges.description: Deletes collinear edges by merging neighboring collinear edges into a single-edge entity.

parameters.name: edge-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of collinear edges to merge. Neighboring collinear edges are merged into one single-edge entity. Argument type: EDGE LIST

sdegeo:delete-contact-boundary-edges.description: Removes the active contact set attribute from all edges of the specified regions.

parameters.name: body | body-list
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies a body or body list. If omitted, the Scheme extension takes the body or bodies from (sde:selected-entities). Only the active contacts are removed. Argument type: BODY | BODY LIST (optional)

sdegeo:delete-contact-boundary-faces.description: Deletes a contact attribute from all faces of the specified body or bodies.

parameters.name: body | body-list
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies a body or body list. If omitted and no face-list is defined, the Scheme extension takes the body or bodies from (sde:selected-entities). The active contact set name is used to specify the contacts. Argument type: BODY | BODY LIST (optional)

sdegeo:delete-contact-edges.description: Deletes the current contact set attributes from the selected edges.

parameters.name: edge | edge-list
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies an edge or edge list. If omitted, the Scheme extension takes the edges from (sde:selected-entities). Only the active contacts are removed. Argument type: EDGE | EDGE LIST (optional)

sdegeo:delete-contact-faces.description: Removes the contact attribute from specified faces.

parameters.name: face | face-list
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies a face or face list. If omitted and face-list is not defined, the Scheme extension takes the faces from (sde:selected-entities). The name of the active contact set is used. Argument type: FACE | FACE LIST (optional)

sdegeo:delete-contact-set.description: Deletes the specified contact set and removes corresponding contact attributes.

parameters.name: contact-name
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of the contact set to delete. The corresponding contact attributes (either 2D contact or 3D contact) are also removed. Argument type: STRING

parameters.name: cs-name
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of the coordinate system to delete. Argument type: STRING

sdegeo:delete-edges.description: Removes the specified edges from the model. Default 90-degree angular tolerance protects features.

parameters.name: edge-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of edges to remove from the model. The command appends matching neighbor edges to provide a conformal result. Only linear edges can be deleted. Applies to 2D models only. Argument type: EDGE LIST

parameters.name: angular-tolerance
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies the angular tolerance in degrees for protecting model features. By default, 90 degrees is used: only edges that enclose an angle larger than this value will be removed. Argument type: REAL (optional)

sdegeo:delete-nearly-collinear-edges.description: Deletes nearly collinear edges where midpoint distance between two edges is closer than dist.

parameters.name: edge-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of nearly collinear edges to remove. Two edges are nearly collinear if the midpoint between them is closer than the specified dist to the line defined by the other two non-common endpoints. Argument type: EDGE LIST

parameters.name: dist
parameters.desc: Specifies the maximum distance threshold. If the distance from the midpoint between two edges to the line defined by the other endpoints is closer than this value, the edges are classified as nearly collinear and removed. Argument type: REAL

sdegeo:delete-region.description: Deletes the specified region(s), including Ref/Eval window bodies, permanently from the model.

parameters.name: bodies
parameters.desc: Specifies the body or bodies to permanently remove from the model, including Ref/Eval window bodies. The body is specified by its entity number. Argument type: BODY | BODY LIST

sdegeo:delete-short-edges.description: Deletes all edges from the list that are shorter than the specified maximum length.

parameters.name: edge-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of edges. An edge is deleted only if its length is shorter than max-edge-length. If there are neighboring regions, shared edges must be selected from all bodies to preserve conformity. Argument type: EDGE LIST

parameters.name: max-edge-length
parameters.desc: Specifies the maximum edge length threshold. Edges shorter than this value are deleted from the model. Argument type: REAL

sdegeo:delete-vertices.description: Removes the specified vertices from the model. Shared vertices must be selected from all bodies.

parameters.name: vertex-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of vertices to remove from the model. If there are neighboring regions, shared vertices must be selected from all bodies to preserve conformity. Only vertices shared by linear edges can be deleted. Applies to 2D models only. Argument type: VERTEX LIST

sdegeo:delete-work-plane.description: Deletes the specified work plane. The global work plane "base" cannot be deleted.

parameters.name: wp-name
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of the work plane to delete. The global work plane 'base' is created automatically and cannot be deleted. Argument type: STRING

sdegeo:fillet.description: Performs 3D edge or vertex rounding with constant-radius fillet.

parameters.name: edge | vertex-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of edges or a list of vertices to be rounded. If an edge list is specified, it must contain all tangent continuous edges; otherwise, rounding fails. Argument type: EDGE LIST | VERTEX LIST

parameters.name: fillet-radius
parameters.desc: Specifies the fillet radius used for the operation. The radius must be selected so that the resulting model is physically (topologically) correct. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: adaptive-filleting
parameters.desc: Optional. If set to #t, adaptive filleting is performed: if the operation fails using the original fillet-radius value, it is repeated with a sequence of operations using smaller radii until it succeeds. Argument type: BOOLEAN (optional)

sdegeo:fillet-2d.description: Rounds the specified vertex or vertices in 2D models.

parameters.name: vertex(s)
parameters.desc: Specifies a vertex or a list of vertices to round. When rounding is performed on a vertex shared by neighboring regions, all vertices must be included in the list. Argument type: ACIS VERTEX | ACIS VERTEX LIST

parameters.name: fillet-radius
parameters.desc: Specifies the fillet radius. Must be selected so that the operation does not result in an invalid geometry. Small roundings can result in an excessive number of small elements during meshing. Argument type: REAL

sdegeo:get-active-work-plane.description: Returns the name of the active work plane as a string.

sdegeo:get-auto-region-naming.description: Returns the status of the automatic region-naming option (#t or #f).

sdegeo:get-contact-edgelist.description: Returns all edges with the specified contact set name as a 2d-contact attribute.

parameters.name: contact-set-name
parameters.desc: Name of the contact set

sdegeo:get-contact-facelist.description: Returns all faces with the specified contact set name as a 3d-contact attribute.

parameters.name: contact-set-name
parameters.desc: Name of the contact set

sdegeo:get-current-contact-set.description: Returns the name of the current (active) contact set.

sdegeo:get-default-boolean.description: Returns the default Boolean behavior: "AB", "ABA", "BAB", "ABiA", "ABiB", or "XX".

sdegeo:get-region-counter.description: Returns the current region counter value used for automatic region naming (Region_N).

sdegeo:set-active-coord-sys.description: Sets the specified coordinate system as the active one for subsequent geometry creation.

parameters.name: cs-name
parameters.desc: Name of the coordinate system to set as active

sdegeo:set-active-work-plane.description: Sets the specified work plane as the active one for subsequent geometry creation.

parameters.name: wp-name
parameters.desc: Name of the work plane to set as active

sdegeo:set-auto-region-naming.description: Switches on or off the automatic region-naming option.

parameters.name: on-off
parameters.desc: Boolean to switch on (#t) or off (#f) automatic region naming

sdegeo:set-contact.description: Attaches the active contact to specified entities. Pass "remove" to detach.

parameters.name: entity | entity-list
parameters.desc: Entity or list of entities

parameters.name: contact-name
parameters.desc: Name of the contact set to activate or create (optional)

parameters.name: "remove"
parameters.desc: Optional; pass "remove" to detach contact

sdegeo:set-contact-boundary-edges.description: Defines all boundary edges of the specified regions as contacts using the active contact set.

parameters.name: body | body-list
parameters.desc: Body or body list

sdegeo:set-contact-boundary-faces.description: Attaches a contact attribute to each face of the specified body using the active contact set.

parameters.name: body | body-list
parameters.desc: Body or body list

sdegeo:set-contact-edges.description: Marks all edges in the list as contacts belonging to the specified contact set.

parameters.name: edge-list
parameters.desc: List of edges to mark as contacts

parameters.name: contact-set-name
parameters.desc: Name of the predefined contact set to assign

sdegeo:set-contact-faces.description: Attaches a contact attribute to specified faces using the active contact set name.

parameters.name: face | face-list
parameters.desc: Face or list of faces

sdegeo:set-contact-faces-by-polygon.description: Imprints polygons on existing faces and attaches a 3D contact attribute to the imprinted segments.

parameters.name: polygon-list
parameters.desc: List of polygons (each polygon is a POSITION list) to imprint and assign contact

parameters.name: normal-list
parameters.desc: List of normal vectors, one per polygon, specifying the direction of the imprint

parameters.name: contact-set-name
parameters.desc: Name of the contact set to assign to the imprinted face segments

sdegeo:set-current-contact-set.description: Sets the name of the current (active) contact set.

parameters.name: csName
parameters.desc: Contact set name to make active

sdegeo:set-default-boolean.description: Sets the default Boolean behavior for overlapping regions: "AB", "ABA", "BAB", "ABiA", "ABiB", or "XX".

parameters.name: boolean-value
parameters.desc: Predefined string: "AB" (merge), "ABA" (subtract new from existing), "BAB" (subtract existing from new), "ABiA", "ABiB", or "XX" (allow overlaps)

sdegeo:set-region-counter.description: Sets the region counter explicitly. Next generated region will be Region_N.

parameters.name: counter
parameters.desc: Integer value for the region counter. The next generated region will be named Region_N where N is this counter value

sdegeo:set-region-counter-aut.description: Automatically sets the region counter to the next available number by scanning existing Region_N attributes.

sdegeo:move-2d-regions.description: Moves the specified regions to a new location by the given gvector. For 2D models.

parameters.name: body | body-list
parameters.desc: Body or list of bodies to move

parameters.name: gvector
parameters.desc: Vector specifying the displacement to the new location

sdegeo:move-edge.description: Moves the specified edge to a new location by the given gvector.

parameters.name: edge
parameters.desc: Edge entity (identified by entity ID) to move

parameters.name: gvector
parameters.desc: Vector specifying the displacement to the new location

sdegeo:move-vertex.description: Moves the specified vertex or vertex list to a new location given by a position or gvector.

parameters.name: ve
parameters.desc: Vertex or vertex list (identified by entity IDs) to move

parameters.name: pe
parameters.desc: New position given as a position entity, a gvector (single vertex), or a position list (vertex list)

sdegeo:scale.description: Scales entities with specified factors. Obsolete — use sdegeo:scale-selected instead.

parameters.name: entity-list
parameters.desc: Body, body list, or "all" to scale

parameters.name: scale-x
parameters.desc: Scaling factor along the x-axis

parameters.name: scale-y
parameters.desc: Scaling factor along the y-axis

parameters.name: scale-z
parameters.desc: Scaling factor along the z-axis (optional)

sdegeo:scale-selected.description: Scales entities using a transform:scaling-type transform object.

parameters.name: entity-list
parameters.desc: Body or body list to scale

parameters.name: transform
parameters.desc: Scaling data specified as a transform:scaling-type transform object

sdegeo:sweep.description: Creates a sheet or solid body by sweeping a profile face along a path (wire, distance, vector, or axis).

parameters.name: profile
parameters.desc: Face defining the sweep geometry; becomes the base of the solid or edge of the surface

parameters.name: path | distance | vector | axis
parameters.desc: Sweep definition: wire, numeric distance, gvector, or (position . gvector) axis

parameters.name: sweep-options
parameters.desc: Optional sweep-options data structure created by sweep:options. Defaults: solid, draft angle 0, gap type 2, twist 0, minimum rotation rail law

sdegeo:sweep-corner.description: Performs a sweep producing a shape containing a solid angle, defined by a sweep axis (position + gvector). [信源不充分]

parameters.name: face | face-list
parameters.desc: Face or list of faces to sweep

parameters.name: position
parameters.desc: Start point of the sweep axis

parameters.name: gvector
parameters.desc: Direction of the sweep axis

sdegeo:split-insert-device.description: Splits a device at a specified position and inserts a part between the split bodies. For 2D and 3D devices.

parameters.name: splitpos
parameters.desc: Position at which to split the device

parameters.name: splitdir
parameters.desc: Gvector specifying the direction of the insertion

parameters.name: splitlength
parameters.desc: Length of the part to insert between the split bodies

parameters.name: splitmerge
parameters.desc: If #t, the split faces are merged

sdegeo:split-solid.description: Splits a solid body into two parts using a cutting plane defined by a base position and normal vector. [信源不充分]

parameters.name: solid-body
parameters.desc: Solid body to split

parameters.name: base-position
parameters.desc: Point on the cutting plane

parameters.name: plane-normal
parameters.desc: Normal vector of the cutting plane

sdegeo:translate.description: Translates entities by the specified xyz distance. Obsolete — use sdegeo:translate-selected instead.

parameters.name: entity-list
parameters.desc: Body, body list, or "all" to translate

parameters.name: translate-x
parameters.desc: Translation distance along the x-axis of the active coordinate system

parameters.name: translate-y
parameters.desc: Translation distance along the y-axis of the active coordinate system

parameters.name: translate-z
parameters.desc: Translation distance along the z-axis of the active coordinate system (optional)

sdegeo:translate-selected.description: Translates entities using a transform:translation-type transform object. Supports repeat and overlap control.

parameters.name: entity-list
parameters.desc: Body or body list to translate

parameters.name: transform
parameters.desc: Translation data specified as a transform:translation-type transform object

parameters.name: keep-flag
parameters.desc: If #t and translated body overlaps original, overlap is removed using default rule

parameters.name: repeat-number
parameters.desc: Number of times to repeat the translation (optional)

sdegeo:2d-cut.description: Extracts a 2D rectangular part from a 2D device, specified by two opposite corners.

parameters.name: p1
parameters.desc: Specifies one of the opposite corners of the rectangle. Argument type: POSITION

parameters.name: p2
parameters.desc: Specifies the other opposite corner of the rectangle. Argument type: POSITION

sdegeo:3d-cut.description: Cuts a 2D slice from a 3D device, defined by a base point and a normal vector to the cut plane.

parameters.name: base-position
parameters.desc: Specifies the base point used to define the cutplane. Argument type: POSITION

parameters.name: normal-vector
parameters.desc: Specifies the normal vector to the cutplane. Argument type: GVECTOR

parameters.name: tr-xy
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies whether to transform the 2D cut to the xy plane. This is needed when the 2D cut is to be meshed. The default is #f. Argument type: BOOLEAN

parameters.name: tr-record
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies whether to record the transform (rotation and translation) that transforms the 2D cut on the xy plane. The sde:tr-get Scheme extension can be used to return the rotation and translation components of the transform. The default is #f. Argument type: BOOLEAN

parameters.name: tr-axisaligned
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies whether to trigger new transformation rules for axis-aligned cuts. If the new transformation rule is applied, then the positioning of the 2D cut can be calculated explicitly following simple rules. The default is #f. Argument type: BOOLEAN

sdegeo:average-edge-length.description: Computes the length of each edge in the edge list and returns the average edge length.

parameters.name: edge-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of edges whose lengths are computed and averaged. Argument type: EDGE LIST

sdegeo:body-trim.signature: (sdegeo:body-trim x0 y0 z0 x1 y1 z1)
sdegeo:body-trim.description: Trims all bodies to the specified box, removing parts that lie outside.

parameters.name: x0
parameters.desc: Minimum x-coordinate of the trim box

parameters.name: y0
parameters.desc: Minimum y-coordinate of the trim box

parameters.name: z0
parameters.desc: Minimum z-coordinate of the trim box

parameters.name: x1
parameters.desc: Maximum x-coordinate of the trim box

parameters.name: y1
parameters.desc: Maximum y-coordinate of the trim box

parameters.name: z1
parameters.desc: Maximum z-coordinate of the trim box

sdegeo:break-nearly-axis-aligned-edges.description: Breaks nearly axis-aligned linear edges into horizontal or vertical components by inserting additional vertices and staircase edges. Applies to 2D models only.

parameters.name: edge-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of nearly axis-aligned edges to break into horizontal or vertical components. Argument type: EDGE LIST

parameters.name: angular-tolerance
parameters.desc: Specifies the minimal angle in degrees permitted between model edges and the x-axis and y-axis. If the angle is smaller than this value, the edge will be split into a horizontal and vertical component. Default is 5 degrees. Argument type: REAL (optional)

sdegeo:chull2d.description: Computes the 2D convex hull of the specified position list and returns it as a POSITION LIST. Input must be at the xy plane with z=0.

parameters.name: position-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of positions for which the 2D convex hull is computed. The input positions must be defined at the xy plane, and the z-coordinate must be 0. Argument type: POSITION LIST

sdegeo:contact-sets.description: Returns the names of all the defined contact sets as a Scheme list.

sdegeo:curve-intersect.description: Returns the intersection positions of the specified curves.

parameters.name: edge1
parameters.desc: Specifies the first curve (edge entity). Argument type: EDGE

parameters.name: edge2
parameters.desc: Specifies the second curve (edge entity). Argument type: EDGE

sdegeo:define-3d-contact-by-polygon.description: Defines a 3D face contact by imprinting a general polygon wire to an existing body, splitting its faces to create a polygonal contact area.

parameters.name: pos-list
parameters.desc: Specifies the vertices of the imprinted polygon. The polygon must be closed, so the first position must be repeated as the last position. The imprint splits the faces of the body. Argument type: POSITION LIST

parameters.name: dir-vector
parameters.desc: Specifies the direction in which the polygon is projected for the imprint step. Argument type: GVECTOR

parameters.name: contact-name
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of the contact set. Argument type: STRING

sdegeo:define-contact-set.description: Defines a contact set name and initializes the supporting data (edge thickness, color, and face pattern).

parameters.name: name
parameters.desc: Specifies the contact set name. If the name is already defined, the existing contact set is replaced. Argument type: STRING

parameters.name: edgeThickness
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies the edge thickness for 2D edge contacts. Default is 4.0. Argument type: INTEGER (optional)

parameters.name: Color
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies the RGB color as a color object, e.g. (color:rgb 0 1 0). Takes three real-number arguments, each in the range [0, 1]. Default is (color:rgb 1.0 0.0 0.0). Argument type: COLOR (optional)

parameters.name: facePattern
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies the face pattern for 3D face contacts. Can be one of: "solid", "###", "||", "==", "//", "::", "<><", "[]". Default is "##". Argument type: STRING (optional)

sdegeo:define-coord-sys.description: Creates a new coordinate system in the active work plane, with origin at (x-pos, y-pos) relative to the work plane origin, rotated by angle degrees.

parameters.name: cs-name
parameters.desc: Specifies the coordinate system name. The newly created coordinate system is called awp_cs-name. Argument type: STRING

parameters.name: x-pos
parameters.desc: Specifies the x-coordinate of the origin, relative to the origin of the active work plane. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: y-pos
parameters.desc: Specifies the y-coordinate of the origin, relative to the origin of the active work plane. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: angle
parameters.desc: Specifies the rotation angle in degrees. Counterclockwise rotation is positive (looking from the positive z-axis of the active work plane). Argument type: REAL

sdegeo:define-work-plane.description: Creates a new work plane with the given name, origin, x-axis direction, and y-axis direction; z-axis is defined by the right-hand rule.

parameters.name: wp-name
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of the work plane. Argument type: STRING

parameters.name: origin
parameters.desc: Specifies the origin of the work plane. The origin must be considered when defining the x-axis and y-axis. Argument type: POSITION

parameters.name: x-axis
parameters.desc: Specifies a point towards which the x-axis points (relative to the origin). Argument type: POSITION

parameters.name: y-axis
parameters.desc: Specifies a point towards which the y-axis points. The z-axis is defined by the right-hand rule. Argument type: POSITION

sdegeo:del-short-edges.description: Deletes linear edges shorter than the specified edge-length; ridges can be protected by angular-tolerance based on vertex normal difference. Applies to 2D models only.

parameters.name: entity
parameters.desc: Specifies the entity list from which short edges are removed. Argument type: ENTITY LIST

parameters.name: edge-length
parameters.desc: Specifies the edge length threshold. Linear edges shorter than this value are deleted. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: angular-tolerance
parameters.desc: Specifies the angular tolerance for ridge protection. If the vertex normal difference is larger than this value at a vertex location, the vertex and adjoining edges are protected from deletion, even if the edge length is shorter than edge-length. Argument type: REAL

sdegeo:distance.description: Returns the distance between two vertices.

parameters.name: vertex-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of two vertices. The distance between them is returned. Argument type: VERTEX LIST

sdegeo:dnce.description: Deletes nearly collinear edges from a 2D model.

parameters.name: angular-tolerance
parameters.desc: Specifies the angular tolerance in degrees. If the edge normals differ less than this value at a given vertex, the two neighboring edges are deleted and replaced by a linear edge connecting the other two vertices. Argument type: REAL

sdegeo:extend-device.description: Extends a device around its axis-aligned perimeter (2D only).

parameters.name: "right"
parameters.desc: Keyword specifying the right direction for extension. At least one keyword must be used. Argument type: KEYWORD

parameters.name: right
parameters.desc: Specifies the extension distance to the right. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: "left"
parameters.desc: Keyword specifying the left direction for extension. Argument type: KEYWORD

parameters.name: left
parameters.desc: Specifies the extension distance to the left. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: "top"
parameters.desc: Keyword specifying the top direction for extension. Argument type: KEYWORD

parameters.name: top
parameters.desc: Specifies the extension distance to the top. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: "bottom"
parameters.desc: Keyword specifying the bottom direction for extension. Argument type: KEYWORD

parameters.name: bottom
parameters.desc: Specifies the extension distance to the bottom. Argument type: REAL

sdegeo:face-find-interior-point.description: Finds an interior point on a face and returns a position object.

parameters.name: face
parameters.desc: Specifies a face entity. The function returns a position object for an interior point of that face. Argument type: FACE

sdegeo:find-closest-edge.description: Finds the entity ID of the closest edge to the specified position.

parameters.name: position
parameters.desc: Specifies a reference position. The function searches through the edge list of all defined geometric bodies and returns the entity ID of the closest edge. Argument type: POSITION

sdegeo:find-closest-face.description: Finds the entity ID of the closest face to the specified position.

parameters.name: position
parameters.desc: Specifies a reference position. The function searches through the face list of all defined geometric bodies and returns the entity ID of the closest face. Argument type: POSITION

sdegeo:find-closest-vertex.description: Finds the entity ID of the closest vertex to the specified position.

parameters.name: position
parameters.desc: Specifies a reference position. The function searches through the vertex list of all defined geometric bodies and returns the entity ID of the closest vertex. Argument type: POSITION

sdegeo:find-touching-faces.description: Finds all touching faces from a given face or face list; returns face pairs of input face and its neighbor.

parameters.name: flist
parameters.desc: Specifies a face or a list of faces. Returns a list of pairs containing the input face entity ID and the entity ID of the neighboring touching face, only for faces that have neighbors. Argument type: FACE | FACE LIST

sdegeo:find-touching-faces-global.description: Finds all touching faces globally; returns a list of neighboring face IDs for each input face that has neighbors.

parameters.name: flist
parameters.desc: Specifies a face or a list of faces. Returns a face list containing the neighboring touching face IDs, only for faces that have neighbors. Argument type: FACE | FACE LIST

sdegeo:imprint-circular-wire.description: Splits boundary faces by imprinting a circular wire to the model.

parameters.name: center-position
parameters.desc: Defines the center of the circular wire body

parameters.name: radius
parameters.desc: Radius of the circular patch

sdegeo:imprint-contact.description: Imprints all 3D face contacts to the neighboring touching faces.

sdegeo:imprint-polygonal-wire.description: Splits boundary faces by imprinting a polygonal wire to the model.

parameters.name: vertex-list
parameters.desc: Vertices of the imprinted polygon. The polygon must be closed, so the first position must be repeated as the last position

sdegeo:imprint-rectangular-wire.description: Splits boundary faces by imprinting a rectangular wire to the model.

parameters.name: position1
parameters.desc: One of two opposite corners of the imprinted rectangular body

parameters.name: position2
parameters.desc: The other of two opposite corners of the imprinted rectangular body

sdegeo:imprint-triangular-wire.description: Splits boundary faces by imprinting a triangular wire to the model.

parameters.name: position1
parameters.desc: First vertex of the imprinted triangular wire

parameters.name: position2
parameters.desc: Second vertex of the imprinted triangular wire

parameters.name: position3
parameters.desc: Third vertex of the imprinted triangular wire

sdegeo:insert-vertex.description: Inserts a vertex in a 2D model by projecting the specified insert position to the nearest edge.

parameters.name: vertex-list
parameters.desc: List of vertices to insert into the 2D model

sdegeo:max-edge-length.description: Returns the length of the longest edge in the given edge list.

parameters.name: edge-list
parameters.desc: List of edges to compute lengths for

sdegeo:min-edge-length.description: Returns the length of the shortest edge in the given edge list.

parameters.name: edge-list
parameters.desc: List of edges to compute lengths for

sdegeo:point-entity-relationship.description: Returns the containment relationship between a body and a point: "inside", "boundary", or "outside".

parameters.name: body
parameters.desc: Body to test containment against

parameters.name: lposition
parameters.desc: The position to test

sdegeo:polygonal-split.description: Splits a 2D device along the specified path defined by a position list.

parameters.name: plist
parameters.desc: Position list defining the split path. First and last positions snap to the closest boundary edge

sdegeo:prune-vertices.description: Merges neighboring edges by pruning vertices whose edge normals are within the angular tolerance (2D models only).

parameters.name: body-list | edge-list | vertex-list
parameters.desc: The list of bodies, edges, or vertices on which to operate

parameters.name: angular-tolerance
parameters.desc: Angular threshold in degrees. If edge normals at a common vertex differ by less than this value, the edges merge by removing the common vertex

sdegeo:ray-test.description: Returns the positions where a ray intersects a solid as pairs of (ENTITY . POSITION), sorted along the ray direction.

parameters.name: entity
parameters.desc: Solid body to test ray intersection against

parameters.name: ray
parameters.desc: Ray defined by a position and a direction

parameters.name: radius
parameters.desc: Search radius for the ray intersection test

parameters.name: oldContName
parameters.desc: Name of the contact set to rename

parameters.name: newContName
parameters.desc: New name for the contact

sdegeo:skin-wires.description: Creates a sheet body that interpolates a series of wires or faces.

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: List of wire bodies or faces defining cross sections to be interpolated (at least two required)

parameters.name: path
parameters.desc: Optional wire defining a curve intersected with each profile plane; tangent vectors at intersection points constrain the surface

parameters.name: skin-options
parameters.desc: Optional skinning options data structure (see skin:options)

sdegeo:skin-wires-guides.description: Creates a sheet body that interpolates a series of wires or faces with a guide curve.

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: List of wire bodies or faces defining cross sections to be interpolated

parameters.name: guides
parameters.desc: List of edge curves that the skinning surface directly follows in the skinning direction. Must intersect each wire profile and be C1 continuous

parameters.name: skin-options
parameters.desc: Optional skinning options data structure (see skin:options)

sdegeo:skin-wires-normal.description: Creates a sheet body that interpolates a series of wires or faces with take-off vectors normal to the plane of the wire body.

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: List of wire bodies or faces defining cross sections to be interpolated (at least two required)

parameters.name: normal-type
parameters.desc: String: "first_normal", "last_normal", "ends_normal", or "all_normal". Specifies which profiles receive the normal take-off constraint

parameters.name: skin-options
parameters.desc: Optional skinning options data structure (see skin:options)

sdegeo:skin-wires-vectors.description: Creates a sheet body that interpolates a series of wires with take-off vectors as constraints.

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: List of wire or face bodies defining cross sections to be interpolated (at least two required)

parameters.name: SPAvector-list
parameters.desc: List of gvector take-off vectors controlling the direction the skin surface leaves each profile

parameters.name: guide-list
parameters.desc: Optional list of guide edges the surface follows

parameters.name: magnitude-list
parameters.desc: Optional list of real magnitudes controlling take-off vector strengths

parameters.name: skin-options
parameters.desc: Optional skinning options data structure (see skin:options)

sdegeo:taper-faces.description: Tapers an array of faces about a point and a supplied draft vector by a given draft angle.

parameters.name: face-list
parameters.desc: List of faces of the body to be tapered

parameters.name: point
parameters.desc: Position on the taper plane

parameters.name: normal
parameters.desc: Gvector specifying the normal of the taper plane at the given point; defines the direction of the angle

parameters.name: angle
parameters.desc: Rotation angle in degrees for the taper

sdegeo:vsmooth.description: Two-dimensional boundary smoothing using polynomial least squares fit.

parameters.name: polynomial-degree
parameters.desc: Polynomial order for least squares fit: 2 (quadratic) or 3 (cubic)

parameters.name: vertex-list
parameters.desc: List of vertices to smooth. Use (entity:vertices (get-body-list)) for all model vertices

sde:min-x.description: Returns the minimum x-coordinate of the specified body or body-list.

parameters.name: body | body-list
parameters.desc: A body or body-list to query.

sde:min-y.description: Returns the minimum y-coordinate of the specified body or body-list.

parameters.name: body | body-list
parameters.desc: A body or body-list to query.

sde:min-z.description: Returns the minimum z-coordinate of the specified body or body-list.

parameters.name: body | body-list
parameters.desc: A body or body-list to query.

sde:pick-point-on-wp.description: Selects a point on the view window and returns its global xyz coordinates as a position entity.

sde:pick-two-points-on-wp.description: Selects two points on the view window and returns their global xyz coordinates as a list of two position entities.

sde:save-model.description: Saves all information about a model including geometry (ACIS .sat), mesh commands, defined parameters, and TDR boundary file.

parameters.name: file-name
parameters.desc: Specifies the base file name for saving model files. The extension saves a native ACIS .sat file for geometry and Ref/Eval windows, a mesh command file with refinement and doping information, a Scheme file with all defined parameters, and a TDR boundary file. Argument type: STRING

sde:save-parameters.description: Saves all defined parameters (Scheme variables created using sde:define-parameter) to a .scm file.

parameters.name: file-name
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of a file in which to save all of the defined parameters. The saved .scm file can be edited and used for later sessions to restore the complete previous state of the modeler. Some GUI information (such as size and background color) is also recorded. Argument type: STRING

sde:scmwin-get-font-families.description: Returns the available font types used in the command-line window.

sde:scmwin-get-font-family.description: Returns the font type used in the command-line window.

sde:scmwin-get-font-size.description: Returns the font size used in the command-line window.

sde:scmwin-get-font-style.description: Returns the font style as a pair: first value is 0 (Normal), 1 (Italic), or 2 (Oblique); second value is #t (Bold) or #f.

sde:scmwin-select-font.description: Displays the Select Font dialog box for choosing font, font style, and size of the command-line window.

sde:scmwin-set-font-family.description: Sets the font type to be used in the command-line window.

parameters.name: font-type
parameters.desc: Specifies the font type to be used. Use sde:scmwin-get-font-families for available fonts and sde:scmwin-get-font-family for the current setting. Argument type: STRING

sde:scmwin-set-font-size.description: Sets the font size to be used in the command-line window.

parameters.name: font-size
parameters.desc: Specifies the font size to be used. Argument type: INTEGER

sde:scmwin-set-prefs.description: Sets the preferences of the command-line window including font type, font style, font size, and window height.

parameters.name: font-type
parameters.desc: Specifies the command-line window font. Use sde:scmwin-get-font-families for the available fonts and sde:scmwin-get-font-family for the active setting. Argument type: STRING

parameters.name: font-style
parameters.desc: Sets the actual font style. Argument type: STRING

parameters.name: font-size
parameters.desc: Sets the font size. Argument type: INTEGER

parameters.name: cmdwin-height
parameters.desc: Specifies the height of the command-line window. Argument type: INTEGER

sde:scmwin-set-window-height.description: Sets the height (in pixels) of the command-line window.

parameters.name: height
parameters.desc: Specifies the height of the command-line window. Argument type: INTEGER

sde:scmwin-suppress-output.description: Displays or hides messages in the command-line window.

parameters.name: bool
parameters.desc: Specifies whether to show or hide messages in the command-line window. Argument type: BOOLEAN

sde:scmwin-get-window-height.description: Returns the height of the command-line window in terms of number of lines (or rows).

sde:selected-entities.description: Returns the currently active selected geometric entities (body, face, edge, vertex) in a Scheme list. The selection tools of the GUI (Select mode) can be used to select geometric entities; selected entities are highlighted.

sde:selected-refeval-windows.description: Returns the names of the currently selected Ref/Eval windows in a Scheme list. The selection tools of the GUI (Select mode) can be used to select previously defined Ref/Eval windows; selected windows are highlighted.

sde:check-3d-license-status.description: Checks the status of the 3D license, returns #t or #f depending on whether a 3D license is checked out.

sde:check-model.description: Performs a detailed model check and reports problems to a report file.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.desc: Optional report file name, default is sde-model-debug.log

sde:create-bg-image.description: Loads a GIF image file into Sentaurus Structure Editor (GIF87a format only).

parameters.name: filename
parameters.desc: Path to the GIF image file

parameters.name: horizontal-size
parameters.desc: Horizontal GUI size of the image

parameters.name: vertical-size
parameters.desc: Vertical GUI size of the image

sde:create-dialog.description: Creates and registers a dialog box object, returns the dialog entity ID.

parameters.name: dlg-name
parameters.desc: Name/title of the dialog box

sde:delay-graphics-update.description: Switches on or off the automatic refreshing of the view window.

parameters.name: flag
parameters.desc: #t to suppress graphics update, #f to enable and flush buffer

sde:delay-graphics-update?.description: Returns the current status of sde:delay-graphics-update.

sde:delete-bg-image.description: Removes the previously loaded image from memory.

sde:delete-materials.description: Deletes the geometric bodies with the specified DATEX material.

parameters.name: materials
parameters.desc: DATEX material name or list of DATEX material names

parameters.name: parameter-name
parameters.desc: Name of the parameter to delete

sde:dialog-add-input.description: Adds an input field to a dialog box.

parameters.name: dlgid
parameters.desc: Dialog object returned by sde:create-dialog

parameters.name: varname
parameters.desc: Scheme variable name passed to the executed script

parameters.name: inputstrfield
parameters.desc: Label text displayed in the input dialog

parameters.name: inputtype
parameters.desc: Type of input: "STRING", "INTEGER", or "REAL"

parameters.name: default-value
parameters.desc: Optional default value

sde:dialog-add-pixmap.description: Adds a bitmap image to a dialog box.

parameters.name: dlgid
parameters.desc: Dialog object returned by sde:create-dialog

parameters.name: bmpfilename
parameters.desc: Filename of the bitmap image

sde:dialog-delete.description: Removes a dialog box from memory.

parameters.name: dlgid
parameters.desc: Dialog object returned by sde:create-dialog

sde:dialog-ok-command.description: Binds the OK button of a dialog box to a predefined Scheme function.

parameters.name: dlgid
parameters.desc: Dialog object returned by sde:create-dialog

parameters.name: schemefnname
parameters.desc: Name of the Scheme function to call when OK is clicked

parameters.name: argument-list
parameters.desc: List of argument names in double quotes, passed to the Scheme function

parameters.name: dlgid
parameters.desc: Dialog object returned by sde:create-dialog

sde:display.description: Displays a string in the command-line window; requires newline to flush buffer.

parameters.name: string
parameters.desc: String to display

sde:display-err.description: Writes a string in a terminal window; newline is appended automatically.

parameters.name: string
parameters.desc: String to write to the terminal window

sde:display-std.description: Writes a string in a terminal window; newline is appended automatically.

parameters.name: string
parameters.desc: String to write to the terminal window

sde:fix-imprint.description: Generates a conformal model by imprinting the model edges and faces to neighboring bodies.

sde:fix-orientation.description: Modifies the orientation of 3D bodies so that surface normals point inside the body.

sde:get-backwards-compatibility.description: Returns the value of the given backward-compatibility parameter.

parameters.name: backcompat-param
parameters.desc: The name of a backward-compatibility parameter

sde:get-default-material.description: Returns the active DATEX material as a string.

sde:get-view-params.description: Records the current GUI view settings (eye position, target, up-vector, width, height, projection) into a list.

sde:gui-get-integer.description: Displays a dialog box to enter an integer variable; returns the integer or #f on cancel.

parameters.name: variable-name
parameters.desc: The variable name displayed in the dialog

parameters.name: default-value
parameters.desc: Default integer value

parameters.name: dialog-name
parameters.desc: Optional name for the input dialog box

sde:gui-get-real.description: Displays a dialog box to enter a real variable; returns the real number or #f on cancel.

parameters.name: variable-name
parameters.desc: The variable name displayed in the dialog

parameters.name: default-value
parameters.desc: Default real value

parameters.name: dialog-name
parameters.desc: Optional name for the input dialog box

sde:gui-get-string.description: Displays a dialog box to enter a string variable; returns the string or #f on cancel.

parameters.name: variable-name
parameters.desc: The variable name displayed in the dialog

parameters.name: default-value
parameters.desc: Default string value

parameters.name: dialog-name
parameters.desc: Optional name for the input dialog box

sde:hide.description: Removes the specified entity or entities from the view window.

parameters.name: entity | entity-list
parameters.desc: An entity or list of entities to hide

sde:hide-bg-image.description: Hides the previously loaded background image.

sde:hide-contact.description: Removes from the view all regions that have the specified contact attributes.

parameters.name: contact-name | contact-name-list
parameters.desc: Contact attribute name or list of names

sde:hide-interface.description: Removes from the view all regions that have interfaces with the specified attributes.

parameters.name: interface-name | interface-name-list
parameters.desc: Interface attribute name or list of names

sde:hide-mask.description: Removes the specified masks from the view window.

parameters.name: mask-name | mask-name-list
parameters.desc: Mask name or list of mask names

sde:hide-material.description: Removes regions with the specified DATEX material attributes from the view window.

parameters.name: datex-material | datex-material-list
parameters.desc: DATEX material name or list of names

sde:hide-region.description: Removes the specified regions from the view window.

parameters.name: region-name | region-name-list
parameters.desc: Region name or list of region names

sde:hide-ruler.description: Hides all user-defined rulers in the GUI.

sde:max-x.description: Returns the largest x-dimension of the specified body or body-list.

parameters.name: body | body-list
parameters.desc: A body or body-list to query

sde:max-y.description: Returns the largest y-dimension of the specified body or body-list.

parameters.name: body | body-list
parameters.desc: A body or body-list to query

sde:set-background-color.signature: (sde:set-background-color rtop gtop btop rbottom gbottom bbottom)
sde:set-background-color.description: Sets the background color of the GUI with optional gradient (RGB 0-255).

parameters.name: rtop
parameters.desc: Specifies the red value for the top of the background color (0-255). Argument type: INTEGER

parameters.name: gtop
parameters.desc: Specifies the green value for the top of the background color (0-255). Argument type: INTEGER

parameters.name: btop
parameters.desc: Specifies the blue value for the top of the background color (0-255). Argument type: INTEGER

parameters.name: rbottom
parameters.desc: Specifies the red value for the bottom of the background color (0-255). Argument type: INTEGER

parameters.name: gbottom
parameters.desc: Specifies the green value for the bottom of the background color (0-255). Argument type: INTEGER

parameters.name: bbottom
parameters.desc: Specifies the blue value for the bottom of the background color (0-255). If the top and bottom colors differ, a graded background is used; if they match, the background is uniform. Argument type: INTEGER

sde:set-backwards-compatibility.description: Sets the overall backward compatibility to a given release, or sets specific parameters.

parameters.name: backcompat-release
parameters.desc: Specifies the release version, for example "S-2021.06" or "M-2016.12". When called with one parameter only, sets the general backward compatibility to this release version. Argument type: STRING

parameters.name: backcompat-param
parameters.desc: Specifies a backward-compatibility parameter. Available parameters: "Save Part at End" (defaults to #t; set to #f for compatibility with E-2010.12 and earlier where the .sat file is saved before model modification) and "Z Align Polygons" (defaults to #f; set to #t for E-2010.12 and earlier to reverse polygon direction for coplanar polygons whose face normal is not parallel to the z-axis). Argument type: STRING

sde:set-default-material.description: Sets the active DATEX material to the specified value. Also changes the selected DATEX material in the DATEX list.

parameters.name: datex-material
parameters.desc: Specifies one of the DATEX colors/materials, as defined in the datecodes.txt file. Argument type: DATEMAT

sde:set-menubar-font-size.description: Changes the font size of the menu bar of the GUI (suggested value 10-14).

parameters.name: font-size
parameters.desc: Specifies the font size. Suggested values are 10-14. Argument type: INTEGER

sde:set-process-up-direction.description: Specifies the up-direction convention of the coordinate system to be used during process emulation.

parameters.name: up-direction
parameters.desc: Specifies the up direction. Options: "-x" for -x up direction (default), or "+z" for +z up direction. When using the Sentaurus Process-Sentaurus Structure Editor interface, the generated Scheme script will contain instructions to select the correct coordinate system automatically. Argument type: STRING

sde:set-project-name.description: Sets the project name used in the Build Mesh dialog box.

parameters.name: project-name
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of a project, used in the Build Mesh dialog box as the name of the saved mesh. Argument type: STRING

sde:set-rendering-mode.description: Sets the 3D rendering mode: facet, wire, flat, gouraud (smooth), or hidden.

parameters.name: rendering-mode
parameters.desc: Specifies the rendering mode. Options: "facet" (faceted display), "wire" (wireframe), "flat" (flat shading), "gouraud" or "smooth" (smooth shading), "hidden" (hidden line removal). Argument type: STRING

sde:set-selection-level.description: Sets the selection filter to the specified entity type.

parameters.name: filter-type
parameters.desc: Specifies the filter type for all subsequent selection operations. Options: "body" | "face" | "edge" | "vertex" | "other". Only entities corresponding to the specified type will be selected. Argument type: STRING

sde:set-translucency.description: Sets the translucency of entities: #t for translucent, #f for opaque.

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: Specifies an entity or a list of entities to which translucency is applied. Argument type: ENTITY | ENTITY LIST

parameters.name: tflag
parameters.desc: Specifies whether the entities are translucent (#t) or opaque (#f). Argument type: BOOLEAN

sde:set-view-mode.description: Sets the view and performs a zoom extent. Options: ISO, XY, XZ, YZ, X-Y.

parameters.name: view-mode
parameters.desc: Specifies the view mode. Options: "ISO" (isometric view), "XY" (xy plane), "XZ" (xZ plane), "YZ" (yZ plane), "X-Y" (x-axis horizontal, y-axis pointing downwards). A zoom extent operation is also performed. Argument type: STRING

sde:set-view-operator.description: Sets the view operator (toolbar active action): orbit, zoom, pan, or select.

parameters.name: op-mode
parameters.desc: Specifies the operation mode, setting the toolbar active action. Options: "orbit" (rotation), "pan" (panning), "select" (selection mode), or "zoom" (zooming). Argument type: STRING

sde:set-view-params.description: Restores the GUI view from a list of view parameters as returned by sde:get-view-params.

parameters.name: view-params
parameters.desc: Specifies a list containing view parameters: eye (POSITION), target (POSITION), up-vector (GVECTOR, optional), width (REAL, optional), height (REAL, optional), projection (STRING, optional). The order is not significant, but eye must precede target and width must precede height. Missing optional values are extracted from the current camera position. Argument type: LIST

sde:set-window-position.description: Positions the main GUI window at the specified screen coordinates.

parameters.name: xpos
parameters.desc: Specifies the horizontal placement of the GUI on screen. Argument type: INTEGER

parameters.name: ypos
parameters.desc: Specifies the vertical placement of the GUI on screen. Argument type: INTEGER

sde:set-window-size.description: Resizes the main GUI window to the specified dimensions in pixels.

parameters.name: xsize
parameters.desc: Specifies the horizontal size (in pixels) of the GUI. Argument type: INTEGER

parameters.name: ysize
parameters.desc: Specifies the vertical size (in pixels) of the GUI. Argument type: INTEGER

sde:set-window-style.description: Sets the GUI style: Windows, Motif, Cleanlooks, Plastique, or CDE.

parameters.name: wstyle
