parameters.desc: Sets the GUI style. Available styles: "Windows", "Motif", "Cleanlooks", "Plastique", and "CDE". Argument type: STRING

sde:show.description: Adds the specified entity or entities to the view window and refreshes it. If the entity is already visible, there is no change.

parameters.name: entity | entity-list
parameters.desc: Specifies an entity (ENTITY) or a list of entities (ENTITY LIST) to display in the view window

sde:showattribs.description: Shows the SDE attributes (ACIS part number, material, region) attached to bodies.

parameters.name: entity-list | "all"
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of entities (ENTITY_LIST) to inspect, or the keyword "all" to show attributes for all ACIS bodies

sde:show-bg-image.description: Displays the previously loaded background image.

sde:show-contact.description: Adds to the view all regions that have the specified contact attributes. Contacts are identified with the attribute name contact; in 2D, contacts are edges; in 3D, contacts are faces.

parameters.name: contact-name | contact-name-list
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of a contact (STRING) or a list of contact names (STRING LIST) to display

sde:show-grid.description: Switches on or off the grid visualization in the view window.

parameters.name: on-off
parameters.desc: Specifies whether to switch on (#t) or off (#f) visualization of the grid. Argument type: BOOLEAN

sde:show-interface.description: Adds the specified interfaces between neighboring regions to the view window. In 2D, interfaces are edges between neighboring regions; in 3D, interfaces are faces between neighboring regions.

parameters.name: interface-name | interface-name-list
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of an interface (STRING) or a list of interface names (STRING LIST) to display

sde:show-mask.description: Adds the specified masks (planar wire bodies) to the view window.

parameters.name: mask-name | mask-name-list
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of a mask (STRING) or a list of mask names (STRING LIST). Masks are planar wire bodies that have the specified mask names as mask name attributes.

sde:show-material.description: Adds regions with the specified DATEX material names to the view window. If a region is already visible, there is no change.

parameters.name: datex-material | datex-material-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a DATEX material (DATEXMAT) or a list of DATEX materials (DATEXMAT LIST) whose regions to display

sde:show-pcurves.description: Visualizes parametric curves for each entity; affects wire frame view only.

parameters.name: rendering-flag
parameters.desc: Specifies whether to switch on (#t) or off (#f) the parameter line visualization. Only affects the wire frame view. Argument type: BOOLEAN

parameters.name: u-params
parameters.desc: Optional. Number of parameter lines in the u direction. Default is 5 if not specified. Argument type: INTEGER

parameters.name: v-params
parameters.desc: Optional. Number of parameter lines in the v direction. Default is 5 if not specified. Argument type: INTEGER

sde:show-region.description: Adds bodies with the specified region name to the view window. If the region is already visible, there is no change.

parameters.name: region-name | region-name-list
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of a region (STRING) or a list of region names (STRING LIST). Must be existing region names.

sde:max-z.description: Returns the maximum z-coordinate of the specified body or body-list.

parameters.name: body | body-list
parameters.desc: A body or body-list to query

sde:view-filter-reset.description: Resets the view window to the default; all regions and contacts shown, interfaces hidden.

sde:view-set-light-intensity.description: Sets the light intensity of the GUI view window (value between 0 and 1).

parameters.name: lintensity
parameters.desc: Sets the light intensity to a value between 0 and 1. Argument type: INTEGER (manual specifies INTEGER but valid range is 0-1)

sde:view-set-visible-area.description: Resizes the GUI drawing area to the specified bounding box. If the bounding box of the created device is known, it may be convenient to preset the view area to that size.

parameters.name: xmin
parameters.desc: Minimum x-coordinate of the visible area bounding box. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: xmax
parameters.desc: Maximum x-coordinate of the visible area bounding box. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: ymin
parameters.desc: Minimum y-coordinate of the visible area bounding box. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: ymax
parameters.desc: Maximum y-coordinate of the visible area bounding box. Argument type: REAL

sde:window-select-2d.signature: (sde:window-select-2d x-left y-left x-right y-right elist stype)
sde:window-select-2d.description: Returns entities inside the specified 2D window (2D models only).

parameters.name: x-left
parameters.desc: Defines the left x-coordinate of the rectangular selection window. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: y-left
parameters.desc: Defines the bottom y-coordinate of the rectangular selection window. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: x-right
parameters.desc: Defines the right x-coordinate of the rectangular selection window. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: y-right
parameters.desc: Defines the top y-coordinate of the rectangular selection window. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: elist
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of bodies to search, or "all" to use all entities in the model. Argument type: BODY LIST

parameters.name: stype
parameters.desc: Specifies the selection criterion (entity type). Options: "vertex" (returns a vertex list), "edge" (returns an edge list), or "body" (returns a body list). Returns an empty list if no entity is found. Argument type: STRING

sde:window-select-3d.description: Returns entities inside the specified 3D cuboid (3D models only).

parameters.name: pmin
parameters.desc: Defines the minimum corner of the 3D selection cuboid. Argument type: POSITION

parameters.name: pmax
parameters.desc: Defines the maximum corner of the 3D selection cuboid. Argument type: POSITION

parameters.name: elist
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of bodies to search, or "all" to use all entities in the model. Argument type: BODY LIST

parameters.name: stype
parameters.desc: Specifies the selection criterion (entity type). Options: "vertex" (returns a vertex list), "edge" (returns an edge list), "face" (returns a face list), or "body" (returns a body list). Returns an empty list if no entity is found. Argument type: STRING

sde:xshow.description: Adds the specified entities to the view exclusively, hiding all other entities.

parameters.name: entity | entity-list
parameters.desc: Specifies an entity (ENTITY) or a list of entities (ENTITY LIST) to show exclusively. All other entities are removed from the view window.

sde:xshow-contact.description: Exclusively shows regions with the specified contact attributes and removes all other regions. Contacts are identified with the attribute name contact; in 2D, contacts are edges; in 3D, contacts are faces.

parameters.name: contact-name | contact-name-list
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of a contact (STRING) or a list of contact names (STRING LIST) to show exclusively

sde:xshow-interface.description: Exclusively shows regions with the specified interface attributes and removes all other interfaces. In 2D, interfaces are edges between neighboring regions; in 3D, interfaces are faces between neighboring regions.

parameters.name: interface-name | interface-name-list
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of an interface (STRING) or a list of interface names (STRING LIST) to show exclusively

sde:xshow-mask.description: Exclusively adds the specified masks to the view window; all other masks are removed.

parameters.name: mask-name | mask-name-list
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of a mask (STRING) or a list of mask names (STRING LIST). Masks are planar wire bodies with the specified mask names as mask name attributes.

sde:xshow-material.description: Exclusively shows regions with the specified DATEX material names. All other regions are removed from the view window.

parameters.name: datex-material | datex-material-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a DATEX material (DATEXMAT) or a list of DATEX materials (DATEXMAT LIST) to show exclusively

sde:xshow-region.description: Exclusively shows bodies with the specified region names. All other bodies are removed from the view window.

parameters.name: region-name | region-name-list
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of a region (STRING) or a list of region names (STRING LIST). The region name(s) must exist.

sde:save-tcl-parameters.description: Saves the defined contact colors to a Tcl file, using the contact color assignment syntax of Sentaurus Visual. The saved Tcl file can be loaded into Sentaurus Visual after a TDR file is loaded, so contacts will have the same colors in Sentaurus Visual as in Sentaurus Structure Editor.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of a Tcl file to save contact color definitions. The file contains set_region_prop commands with -color arguments for each defined contact. Argument type: STRING

sde:get-datex-color-scheme.description: [Inferred] Returns the current DATEX color scheme name.

sde:add-material.description: Assigns the specified material and region attributes to a body.

parameters.name: body
parameters.desc: ACIS entity (body) to which material and region attributes are assigned

parameters.name: material-name
parameters.desc: A valid DATEX material name defined in datexcodes.txt

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Region name used in the tessellated boundary output

sde:back-coord.description: Returns the back coordinate of the specified body or body-list. For UCS: min z; for DF-ISE: min x.

parameters.name: body
parameters.desc: A single geometric body

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: A list of geometric bodies

sde:bg-image-transparency.description: Sets the transparency of the loaded background image.

parameters.name: transparency-value
parameters.desc: Transparency value between 0 (opaque) and 1 (fully transparent)

sde:bool-regularise.description: Merges the coplanar faces of the specified 3D bodies.

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: List of bodies whose coplanar faces will be merged

sde:bottom-coord.description: Returns the bottom coordinate of the specified body or body-list. For UCS: max x; for DF-ISE: min z.

parameters.name: body
parameters.desc: A single geometric body

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: A list of geometric bodies

sde:build-mesh.description: Generates a tessellated TDR boundary output and a mesh command file, then calls Sentaurus Mesh.

parameters.name: options
parameters.desc: Optional meshing options string

parameters.name: file-basename
parameters.desc: Base filename for the output boundary file (_bnd.tdr) and mesh command file (_msh.cmd)

sde:change-datex-color-scheme.description: Selects the DATEX color visualization scheme (first or second color definition from datexcodes.txt).

parameters.name: color-scheme
parameters.desc: 0 for the first DATEXMAT color (default), 1 for the second (typically brighter)

sde:checkout-3d-license.description: Explicitly checks out a 3D license for Sentaurus Structure Editor.

sde:clear.description: Clears the modeler database and restores all settings to their initial values.

sde:define-parameter.description: Defines a Scheme parameter that can be used later in any Scheme command and saved by sde:save-parameters.

parameters.name: parameter-name
parameters.desc: The name of the parameter to define

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: The value assigned to the parameter (REAL or STRING)

parameters.name: min-value
parameters.desc: Optional minimum value for REAL parameters

parameters.name: max-value
parameters.desc: Optional maximum value for REAL parameters

sde:draw-ruler.signature: (sde:draw-ruler "startpos" startpos "alength" alength "adir" adir "startval" startval "endval" endval ["numdiv" numdiv] ["numdiv2" numdiv2] ["npos" npos] ["acolor" acolor] ["tcolor" tcolor])
sde:draw-ruler.description: Draws a user-defined ruler in the GUI with configurable position, length, direction, tick divisions, and colors.

parameters.name: startpos
parameters.desc: Starting position of the ruler in 3D space

parameters.name: alength
parameters.desc: Length of the ruler

parameters.name: adir
parameters.desc: Direction of the ruler: 0=x-axis, 1=y-axis, 2=z-axis

parameters.name: startval
parameters.desc: Start value of the coordinate axis

parameters.name: endval
parameters.desc: End value of the coordinate axis

parameters.name: numdiv
parameters.desc: Number of main tick divisions (default 5)

parameters.name: numdiv2
parameters.desc: Number of subdivisions in each main division (default 2)

parameters.name: npos
parameters.desc: Position of displayed numbers: 0 or 1 (default 1)

parameters.name: acolor
parameters.desc: Axis color: named color (BLACK, BLUE, CYAN, GREEN, MAGENTA, RED, WHITE, YELLOW) or color:rgb

parameters.name: tcolor
parameters.desc: Text color: named color or color:rgb value

sde:dump-non-default-options.description: Saves all nondefault ACIS options to a text file for debugging and problem reporting.

parameters.name: fname
parameters.desc: The file name for the output text file

sde:exact-coords?.description: Checks the status of the Exact Coordinates mode; returns #t if exact coordinates are used, #f otherwise.

sde:extract-tdr-boundary.description: Extracts the boundary from a TDR grid file and saves it as a TDR boundary file using Sentaurus Mesh.

parameters.name: fname
parameters.desc: The TDR grid file name to extract boundary from

parameters.name: file-name
parameters.desc: Optional output file name for the extracted TDR boundary

sde:front-coord.description: Returns the front coordinate of the specified body or body-list. For UCS: max z; for DF-ISE: max x.

parameters.name: body
parameters.desc: A geometric body

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: A list of geometric bodies

sde:ft_scalar.description: Writes a string in a terminal window; used for DoE parameter extraction in Sentaurus Workbench.

parameters.name: string
parameters.desc: The string to write to the terminal window

sde:info.description: Returns the attributes of Sentaurus Structure Editor attached to the specified bodies, including entity number, material, and region attributes.

parameters.name: entity-list
parameters.desc: List of bodies whose attributes are to be displayed

sde:left-coord.description: Returns the left coordinate (minimum y-coordinate) of the specified body or body-list.

parameters.name: body
parameters.desc: A single geometric body

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: A list of geometric bodies

sde:load-sat.description: Loads a native ACIS .sat file into Sentaurus Structure Editor with correct overlap handling according to the active Boolean setting.

parameters.name: file-name
parameters.desc: Path to the ACIS .sat file to load

sde:material-type.description: Returns the group type (Semiconductor, Conductor, or Insulator) for the specified material from the datexcodes.txt file.

parameters.name: material-name
parameters.desc: DATEX material name to query

sde:merge-materials.description: Merges (unites) geometric bodies with the specified DATEX materials and assigns the new material to the resulting united body.

parameters.name: materials
parameters.desc: A DATEX material or list of DATEX materials whose bodies are to be merged

parameters.name: newmaterial
parameters.desc: The DATEX material to assign to the merged body

sde:new-region-name.description: Returns an automatically generated unique region name (region_N) based on the current region counter.

sde:off-lights.description: Switches off the lights in the view window.

sde:offset-mask.description: Offsets (biases) the specified mask by a given distance.

parameters.name: mask-name
parameters.desc: Name of the mask to offset

parameters.name: offset-distance
parameters.desc: Distance by which to offset the mask

sde:on-lights.description: Switches on the lights in the view window.

sde:open-model.description: Loads a complete Sentaurus Structure Editor model, restoring geometry (.sat), parameters (.scm), and mesh commands.

parameters.name: file-name
parameters.desc: Base file name (without extension); loads .sat, .scm, and mesh command files if they exist

sde:part-load.description: Loads a native ACIS .sat file into Sentaurus Structure Editor, with optional automatic overlap handling.

parameters.name: file-name
parameters.desc: Name of the .sat file to load

parameters.name: loadoption
parameters.desc: Optional; #t enables automatic overlap control, #f (default) loads without overlap checking

sde:post-message.description: Displays a dialog box with a message; must click OK to dismiss.

parameters.name: message
parameters.desc: The message text to display in the dialog box

sde:project-name.description: Returns the project name used in the Build Mesh dialog box to save the created mesh file.

sde:refresh.description: Refreshes and rebuilds the view window by discarding and regenerating the entity display list.

sde:rename-regions.description: Automatically renames regions based on spatial ordering of geometric bodies along a specified direction.

parameters.name: entity-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of entities (geometric bodies) to be ordered and renamed. Argument type: ENTITY LIST

parameters.name: direction
parameters.desc: Specifies the direction in which the geometric bodies are ordered. Options: "+x" | "-x" | "+y" | "-y" | "+z" | "-z". Bodies are ordered based on their minimal bounding box coordinates in the specified direction and assigned region names "region_RC" where RC is a numeric counter starting from 0. For "+x", the body with the smallest minimal bounding box x-coordinate gets "region_0"; for "-x", bodies are ordered by largest x-coordinates in decreasing sequence. Similar rules apply to y and z directions.

sde:restore-cursor.description: Reverts the pointer to the default pointer.

sde:right-coord.description: Returns the right coordinate (maximum y-coordinate) of the specified body or body-list.

parameters.name: body
parameters.desc: Specifies a body to be queried. Argument type: BODY

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of bodies to be queried. Argument type: BODY LIST

sde:scale-scene.description: Scales the model in the view window (visual only, model coordinates unchanged).

parameters.name: xs
parameters.desc: X-axis scale factor. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: ys
parameters.desc: Y-axis scale factor. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: zs
parameters.desc: Z-axis scale factor. Argument type: REAL

sde:separate-lumps.description: Separates disjoint lumps in bodies so each gets a unique region name, required before TDR boundary file generation.

parameters.name: body
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of a single body to separate. Optional; if omitted and no body-list is given, all geometric bodies are separated. Argument type: BODY

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of bodies to separate. Optional; if the argument list is empty, all geometric bodies are separated. Argument type: BODY LIST

sde:setrefprops.description: Sets the refinement properties for the internal faceter that tessellates curved boundaries before TDR output. Deprecated; use the aspect-ratio, max-edge-length, normal-tolerance, and surface-tolerance arguments of sdeio:save-tdr-bnd instead.

parameters.name: surface-tolerance
parameters.desc: Specifies the surface tolerance as an absolute number; the allowable maximum distance between the original model and tessellated output. A smaller value means the tessellated model deviates less from the original. Applies globally to the complete model. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: normal-tolerance
parameters.desc: Specifies the normal tolerance; the allowable maximum difference between the surface normals of the original and tessellated models. For thin curved channels, normal tolerance control is preferred over surface tolerance. Applies globally to the complete model. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: aspect-ratio
parameters.desc: Optional. Controls the maximum aspect ratio of the triangles in the tessellated boundary, preventing sliver faces (triangles with a close-to-zero angle). Recommended value: 2-4. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: max-edge-length
parameters.desc: Optional. Controls the maximum allowable edge length in the tessellated boundary. Can help create a more regular tessellation for meshing, but a small value may cause unnecessary overrefinement. Argument type: REAL

sde:setup-grid.description: Sets the basic grid parameters including spacing, line thickness, and line pattern.

parameters.name: x-spacing
parameters.desc: Specifies the horizontal grid size. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: y-spacing
parameters.desc: Specifies the vertical grid size. Argument type: REAL

parameters.name: edge-weight
parameters.desc: Specifies the line thickness. Argument type: INTEGER

parameters.name: edge-pattern
parameters.desc: Specifies the line pattern used to draw the grid. Possible values: "----" (solid), "- - " (dashed), ". . ." (dotted), "-.-." (dash and dot alternating), "-.-.-." (dash and two dots), "-.-.-.-." (dash and three dots), "------ ------" (long dashes), "center" (very long dash and short dash alternating), "phantom" (very long dash and two short dashes alternating). Argument type: STRING

sde:split-solid.description: Splits a solid (2D and 3D) into separate bodies along a cut plane defined by a base position and normal vector. After splitting, region names are no longer unique (all split parts share the same region name), so renaming may be necessary.

parameters.name: solid-body
parameters.desc: Specifies a body to be split. The split bodies can be treated as high-level entities (deleted, assigned new material/region properties, geometry operations applied). Argument type: BODY

parameters.name: base-position
parameters.desc: Defines the base position (a position variable) for the cut plane. Argument type: POSITION

parameters.name: plane-normal
parameters.desc: Defines the normal vector (a gvector variable) for the cut plane. Argument type: GVECTOR

sde:substring.description: Returns a substring of the input string, from character position min to max (0-indexed). If the string length is smaller than max, an empty string is returned.

parameters.name: string
parameters.desc: Specifies an input string. Argument type: STRING

parameters.name: min
parameters.desc: Specifies the first character position (starting from zero). Argument type: INTEGER

parameters.name: max
parameters.desc: Specifies the last character position. If the string length is smaller than this value, an empty string is returned. Argument type: INTEGER

sde:test-entity.description: Returns the entity type: "unknown", "solid" (positive volume manifold), "non-manifold" (zero volume), or "mask".

parameters.name: entities
parameters.desc: Specifies an entity (ENTITY) or a list of entities (ENTITY LIST) to test. Returns "unknown", "solid" (solid with positive volume, manifold), "non-manifold" (solid with zero volume, non-manifold), or "mask" (same conditions as non-manifold but also has the maskname attribute defined).

sde:toggle-lights.description: Switches on or off the lights of the view window, behaving like a light switch.

sde:top-coord.description: Returns the top coordinate of the specified body or body-list. For UCS: min x; for DF-ISE: max z.

parameters.name: body
parameters.desc: Specifies a body to be queried. Argument type: BODY

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of bodies to be queried. Argument type: BODY LIST

sde:tr-get.description: Returns the 3x3 rotation matrix and 3D translation vector used to place the 3D cut on the xy plane via sdegeo:3d-cut. Returns 12 real numbers: first 9 are the rotation matrix (A), last 3 are the translation (T). Any point in the cut can be calculated by P_cut = P_original x A + T.

sde:wait-cursor.description: Changes the pointer to an hourglass pointer.

sde:zoom-all.description: Fits the model to the view window.

sde:stripextension.description: Deletes the file extension from a string by locating the last dot (.) and removing all characters that follow. If the input string has no dot, the input string is returned unchanged.

parameters.name: name
parameters.desc: Specifies an input string from which to strip the file extension. Argument type: STRING

sdedr:append-cmd-file.description: Appends a text file to the end of the mesh command file. The mesh command file is not created by this Scheme extension. You must call sdedr:write-cmd-file after sdedr:append-cmd-file to create the mesh command file.

parameters.name: file-name
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of a text file to be appended

sdedr:clear.description: Clears all doping-related and refinement-related data from Sentaurus Structure Editor. A subsequent sdedr:write-cmd-file call creates an empty command file.

sdedr:clear-multibox-definitions.description: Deletes all multibox definition-related data from the doping and refinement container.

sdedr:clear-multibox-placements.description: Deletes all multibox placement-related data from the doping and refinement container.

sdedr:clear-profile-definitions.description: Deletes all profile definition-related data from the doping and refinement container.

sdedr:clear-profile-placements.description: Deletes all profile placement-related data from the doping and refinement container.

sdedr:clear-ref-windows.description: Deletes all previously defined Ref/Eval windows from the database.

sdedr:clear-refinement-definitions.description: Deletes all refinement definition-related data from the doping and refinement container. The Definitions section contains the definitions of the refinement entities (definition name, refinement size information).

sdedr:clear-refinement-placements.description: Deletes all refinement placement-related data from the doping and refinement container. The Placements section contains the named placements for refinements (associated refinement windows).

sdedr:clear-submesh-placement-transform.description: Clears all transform operations (Reflect, Rotation, and ShiftVector) as defined for the named external profile placement through sdedr:transform-submesh-placement.

parameters.name: placement-name
parameters.desc: Specifies a placement name to clear all transform operations for

sdedr:convert-mask-to-drs-body.description: Converts the specified mask into a Ref/Eval window. Returns #f if the mask is not found, #t if the conversion is successful.

parameters.name: maskname
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of a mask

parameters.name: refwindowname
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of a Ref/Eval window

sdedr:define-1d-external-profile.signature: (sdedr:define-1d-external-profile name filename "Scale" scale "DataScale" data-scale ["Range" range-from range-to] [lateral-function])
sdedr:define-1d-external-profile.description: Creates a 1D external profile in the mesh command file. The default unit is micrometer. The lateral profile can be given as a Gaussian function or an error function.

parameters.name: name
parameters.desc: Profile definition name

parameters.name: filename
parameters.desc: File containing the 1D profile data

parameters.name: scale
parameters.desc: Coordinate scaling factor to micrometers

parameters.name: data-scale
parameters.desc: Data file scaling factor

parameters.name: range-from
parameters.desc: Optional; start of the range

parameters.name: range-to
parameters.desc: Optional; end of the range

parameters.name: lateral-function
parameters.desc: Optional; specifies the lateral function. "Erf" {"Factor" lateral-factor | "Length" length}, "Eval" {eval-init eval-function}, or "Gauss" {"Factor" lateral-factor | "Length" length | "StdDev" standard-deviation}

sdedr:define-analytical-profile.signature: (sdedr:define-analytical-profile name species initialization function start-value analytic-type [lateral-type-gauss-erf lateral-parameter lateral-value | "Eval" lateral-eval-init lateral-eval-func])
sdedr:define-analytical-profile.description: Creates a user-defined analytic function that describes doping in the mesh command file. The optional definition of lateral diffusion is not available if the definition in the primary direction is the analytic type "General".

parameters.name: name
parameters.desc: Profile definition name

parameters.name: species
parameters.desc: Doping species (e.g. Boron, Phosphorus, Arsenic)

parameters.name: initialization
parameters.desc: Initialization type string

parameters.name: function
parameters.desc: Analytic function expression

parameters.name: start-value
parameters.desc: Default value for the profile

parameters.name: analytical-type
parameters.desc: Specifies the analytic type. "General" includes both primary and lateral distributions; "Eval" for evaluated expressions

parameters.name: lateral-function
parameters.desc: Optional; specifies the lateral function type. "Gauss" | "Erf" with lateral-parameter ("factor" | "length" | "stddev") and lateral-value; or "Eval" with init-string and function-string

sdedr:define-analytical-profile-placemen.signature: (sdedr:define-analytical-profile-placement placement-name definition-name ref-eval-window symmetry replacement evaluation eval-window decay-length ["Gauss"] eval-window-type)
sdedr:define-analytical-profile-placemen.description: Creates a placement for an analytic profile definition in the mesh command file.

parameters.name: placement-name
parameters.desc: Name for this placement

parameters.name: definition-name
parameters.desc: Name of the previously defined analytic profile

parameters.name: ref-eval-window
parameters.desc: Name of the reference evaluation window (baseline)

parameters.name: symmetry
parameters.desc: Specifies the evaluation direction. "Positive" evaluates in the positive direction along the Ref/Eval window normal (2D: right side of baseline vector); "Negative" evaluates in the negative direction; "Both" evaluates on both sides

parameters.name: replacement
parameters.desc: Specifies the type of replacement. "Replace" replaces ALL other previously defined profiles with this doping profile; "NoReplace" adds the new profile to all previously defined profiles; "LocalReplace" replaces only the doping species being defined (same species only)

parameters.name: evaluation
parameters.desc: Specifies the type of evaluation. "Eval" includes the base line/base polygons in evaluation; "NoEval" writes the "notevalline" keyword, instructing Sentaurus Mesh NOT to include the base line in analytic doping evaluation. Use "NoEval" to avoid double-counting at region interfaces

parameters.name: eval-window
parameters.desc: Name of the evaluation window; can refer to a Ref/Eval window, region name, or material name depending on eval-window-type

parameters.name: decay-length
parameters.desc: Corresponds to the "DecayLength" parameter unless the optional "Gauss" is present, in which case it is interpreted as "GaussDecayLength". Activates smoothing at Ref/Eval window boundaries using an error function with the specified inflection length

parameters.name: eval-window-type
parameters.desc: Specifies the type of Ref/Eval window. "region" means eval-window name refers to a region name; "material" refers to a material name; "evalwin" refers to a Ref/Eval window (default)

sdedr:define-body-interface-refwin.description: Defines a refinement window between the interfaces of the specified geometric bodies. If the interface is a polygon (2D wire body), it is converted to an edge list with "_edge_EN" appended to the name. If the interface is a face list (3D), each face is converted to a polygon with "_lump_FN" appended.

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of geometric bodies whose interfaces define the refinement window

parameters.name: ref-eval-window
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of a refinement window to create

sdedr:define-constant-profile.description: Creates a definition for a constant doping profile in the mesh command file.

parameters.name: definition-name
parameters.desc: Profile definition name

parameters.name: species
parameters.desc: Doping species (e.g. Boron, Phosphorus, Arsenic)

parameters.name: concentration
parameters.desc: Constant doping concentration value

sdedr:define-constant-profile-material.description: Creates a constant doping profile placement based on a material in the mesh command file.

parameters.name: placement-name
parameters.desc: Name for this placement

parameters.name: definition-name
parameters.desc: Name of the previously defined constant profile

parameters.name: material
parameters.desc: Material name (DATEXMAT type, e.g. "Silicon")

parameters.name: decay-length
parameters.desc: Optional; corresponds to the "DecayLength" parameter unless "Gauss" is present, in which case it is interpreted as "GaussDecayLength". Activates smoothing at boundaries using an error function with the specified inflection length; the doping value at boundaries is half the specified concentration

parameters.name: replace
parameters.desc: Optional; "Replace" replaces all other previously defined profiles with this doping profile; "LocalReplace" replaces only the doping species being defined

sdedr:define-constant-profile-placement.description: Creates a Ref/Eval window placement of a constant doping profile definition in the mesh command file.

parameters.name: placement-name
parameters.desc: Name for this placement

parameters.name: definition-name
parameters.desc: Name of the previously defined constant profile

parameters.name: ref-win-name
parameters.desc: Name of the Ref/Eval window

parameters.name: decay-length
parameters.desc: Optional; corresponds to the "DecayLength" parameter unless "Gauss" is present, in which case it is interpreted as "GaussDecayLength". Activates smoothing at boundaries using an error function with the specified inflection length; the doping value at boundaries is half the specified concentration

parameters.name: replace
parameters.desc: Optional; "Replace" replaces all other previously defined profiles with this doping profile; "LocalReplace" replaces only the doping species being defined

sdedr:define-constant-profile-region.description: Creates a constant doping profile placement based on a region in the mesh command file.

parameters.name: placement-name
parameters.desc: Name for this placement

parameters.name: definition-name
parameters.desc: Name of the previously defined constant profile

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Name of the region

parameters.name: decay-length
parameters.desc: Optional; corresponds to the "DecayLength" parameter unless "Gauss" is present, in which case it is interpreted as "GaussDecayLength". Activates smoothing at boundaries using an error function with the specified inflection length; the doping value at boundaries is half the specified concentration

parameters.name: replace
parameters.desc: Optional; "Replace" replaces all other previously defined profiles with this doping profile; "LocalReplace" replaces only the doping species being defined

sdedr:define-erf-profile.signature: (sdedr:define-erf-profile definition-name species "SymPos" symmetry-position {"MaxVal" max-value | "Dose" dose} "Junction" junction "ValueAtDepth" value-at-depth "Depth" depth "Length" length "StdDev" standard-deviation [lateral-function])
sdedr:define-erf-profile.description: Creates a definition of an error-function doping profile in the mesh command file.

parameters.name: definition-name
parameters.desc: Profile definition name

parameters.name: species
parameters.desc: Doping species (e.g. Boron, Phosphorus)

parameters.name: symmetry-position
parameters.desc: Inflection point position; distance of the inflection point to the baseline (Ref/Eval window) in um (tagged "SymPos")

parameters.name: max-value
parameters.desc: Maximum concentration in cm^-3 (tagged "MaxVal", mutually exclusive with dose)

parameters.name: dose
parameters.desc: Implant dose in cm^-2 (tagged "Dose", mutually exclusive with max-value)

parameters.name: junction
parameters.desc: Junction depth in um (tagged "Junction")

parameters.name: value-at-depth
parameters.desc: Concentration at junction in cm^-3 (tagged "ValueAtDepth")

parameters.name: depth
parameters.desc: Requested distance from the inflection point to the junction in um (tagged "Depth")

parameters.name: length
parameters.desc: Characteristic diffusion length in um (tagged "Length")

parameters.name: standard-deviation
parameters.desc: Standard deviation of the profile broadening in um (tagged "StdDev")

parameters.name: lateral-function
parameters.desc: Optional; specifies the lateral function. "Erf" {"Factor" lateral-factor | "Length" length}, "Eval" {eval-init eval-function}, or "Gauss" {"Factor" lateral-factor | "Length" length | "StdDev" standard-deviation}

sdedr:define-gaussian-profile.signature: (sdedr:define-gaussian-profile definition-name species "PeakPos" peak-position {"PeakVal" peak-concentration | "Dose" dose} {"ValueAtDepth" concentration-at-depth "Depth" depth | "Length" diffusion-length | "StdDev" standard-deviation} [lateral-function])
sdedr:define-gaussian-profile.description: Creates a definition of a Gaussian-function doping profile in the mesh command file.

parameters.name: definition-name
parameters.desc: Profile definition name

parameters.name: species
parameters.desc: Doping species (e.g. Boron, Phosphorus)

parameters.name: peak-position
parameters.desc: Distance of the peak position to the baseline (Ref/Eval window) in um (tagged "PeakPos")

parameters.name: peak-concentration
parameters.desc: Peak concentration in cm^-3 (tagged "PeakVal", mutually exclusive with dose)

parameters.name: dose
parameters.desc: Implant dose in cm^-2 (tagged "Dose", mutually exclusive with peak-concentration)

parameters.name: concentration-at-depth
parameters.desc: Concentration at the junction depth in cm^-3 (tagged "ValueAtDepth")

parameters.name: depth
parameters.desc: Junction depth in um (tagged "Depth"); the standard deviation is computed automatically when using Junction

parameters.name: diffusion-length
parameters.desc: Diffusion length in um defining the profile broadening (tagged "Length")

parameters.name: standard-deviation
parameters.desc: Standard deviation in um defining the profile broadening (tagged "StdDev")

parameters.name: lateral-function
parameters.desc: Optional; specifies the lateral function. "Erf" {"Factor" lateral-factor | "Length" length}, "Eval" {eval-init eval-function}, or "Gauss" {"Factor" lateral-factor | "Length" length | "StdDev" standard-deviation}

sdedr:define-multibox-placement.description: Defines the placements part of a multibox definition.

parameters.name: placement-name
parameters.desc: Name of the multibox placement

parameters.name: definition-name
parameters.desc: Name of the multibox definition to place

parameters.name: refwindow-name
parameters.desc: Name of the reference window

sdedr:define-multibox-size.signature: (sdedr:define-multibox-size mbox-name max-x max-y [max-z] min-x min-y [min-z] ratio-x ratio-y [ratio-z])
sdedr:define-multibox-size.description: Defines the definitions part of a multibox definition.

parameters.name: mbox-name
parameters.desc: Name of the multibox definition

parameters.name: max-x
parameters.desc: Maximum x-coordinate size of the multibox

parameters.name: max-y
parameters.desc: Maximum y-coordinate size of the multibox

parameters.name: max-z
parameters.desc: Maximum z-coordinate size (optional, for 3D)

parameters.name: min-x
parameters.desc: Minimum x-coordinate size of the multibox

parameters.name: min-y
parameters.desc: Minimum y-coordinate size of the multibox

parameters.name: min-z
parameters.desc: Minimum z-coordinate size (optional, for 3D)

parameters.name: ratio-x
parameters.desc: Refinement ratio in x-direction

parameters.name: ratio-y
parameters.desc: Refinement ratio in y-direction

parameters.name: ratio-z
parameters.desc: Refinement ratio in z-direction (optional, for 3D)

sdedr:define-particle-profile.signature: (sdedr:define-particle-profile name file ["AutoScreeningFactor" auto-screening-factor] ["BoundaryExtension" boundary-extension] ["Divisions" divisions] ["DopingAssignment" doping-assignment] ["Normalization" normalization] ["NumberOfThreads" number-of-threads] ["ScreeningFactor" screening-factor] ["ScreeningScalingFactor" screening-scaling-factor] ["Species" species])
sdedr:define-particle-profile.description: Creates a particle doping profile in the mesh command file.

parameters.name: name
parameters.desc: Name of the particle profile definition

parameters.name: file
parameters.desc: Path to the particle data file

parameters.name: options
parameters.desc: Optional tagged arguments: "AutoScreeningFactor" (BOOLEAN) for automatic screening factor; "BoundaryExtension" (REAL) for boundary extension; "Divisions" (INTEGER) for number of divisions; "DopingAssignment" ("CIC" | "NGP" | "Sano") for doping assignment method; "Normalization" (BOOLEAN); "NumberOfThreads" (INTEGER); "ScreeningFactor" (REAL); "ScreeningScalingFactor" (BOOLEAN); "Species" (STRING) for doping species

sdedr:define-particle-profile-placement.signature: (sdedr:define-particle-profile-placement placement-name definition-name "EvalWindow" eval-window eval-window-type ["Replace" replace | "LocalReplace" local-replace])
sdedr:define-particle-profile-placement.description: Creates a placement for a particle profile definition in the mesh command file. Only "Replace" or "LocalReplace" can be set to true at the same time.

parameters.name: placement-name
parameters.desc: Name of the particle profile placement

parameters.name: definition-name
parameters.desc: Name of the particle profile definition

parameters.name: eval-window
parameters.desc: Name of the evaluation window

parameters.name: eval-window-type
parameters.desc: Type of evaluation window: "region" refers to a region name; "material" refers to a material name; "evalwin" refers to a Ref/Eval window

parameters.name: replace
parameters.desc: Optional; whether to replace all other previously defined profiles with this doping profile (tagged "Replace", mutually exclusive with LocalReplace)

parameters.name: local-replace
parameters.desc: Optional; whether to replace only the doping species being defined (tagged "LocalReplace", mutually exclusive with Replace)

sdedr:define-refeval-window.description: Defines a geometric region that can be used as a Ref/Eval window. Multiple body shapes can be created by listing multiple single shapes. The faces are oriented; the face normal follows the right-hand rule (counterclockwise vertex list for polygons). Returns the ENTITY of the ACIS body Ref/Eval window, or #f if the operation failed.

parameters.name: rfwin-name
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of a Ref/Eval window

parameters.name: shape-type
parameters.desc: Shape type (not case sensitive): "Point" (data is a POSITION), "Line" | "Rectangle" | "Cuboid" (data is two opposite corner POSITIONs; Rectangle and Cuboid are axis-aligned), "Polygon" (data is a POSITION LIST, first and last must be the same), "ComplexPolygon" (data is a list of polygon shapes for multiconnected polygons with holes), "Polyhedron" (data is a list of polygon definitions forming a closed polyhedral solid)

parameters.name: data
parameters.desc: POSITION or POSITION LIST depending on shape type. For multi-shape, use (list single-shape1 single-shape2 ...)

sdedr:define-refinement-function.signature: (sdedr:define-refinement-function definition-name function-name {"MaxLenInt" mat-reg mat-reg value [factor] ["DoubleSide"] ["UseRegionNames"] | "MaxInterval" "Variable" dopant-name "Cmin" cmin "Cmax" cmax ["Scaling" scaling] ["TargetLength" targetLength] ["Rolloff"] | "MaxGradient" value | "MaxTransDiff" value})
sdedr:define-refinement-function.description: Adds a refinement function to the specified refinement.

parameters.name: definition-name
parameters.desc: Name of the refinement definition

parameters.name: function-name
parameters.desc: Name of the function to be used for refinement

parameters.name: function-args
parameters.desc: Function-specific arguments: "MaxLenInt" mat-reg mat-reg value [factor] ["DoubleSide"] ["UseRegionNames"] for maximum length at interface; "MaxInterval" "Variable" dopant "Cmin" cmin "Cmax" cmax ["Scaling" scaling] ["TargetLength" targetLength] ["Rolloff"] for maximum interval based on doping variable; "MaxGradient" value; "MaxTransDiff" value. mat-reg specifies the material name or region name defining the interface

sdedr:define-refinement-material.description: Defines the placements information for a materialwise refinement.

parameters.name: placement-name
parameters.desc: Name of the placement

parameters.name: definition-name
parameters.desc: Name of the refinement definition

parameters.name: material-name
parameters.desc: Material name (DATEXMAT type)

sdedr:define-refinement-placement.description: Creates a refinement placement in the mesh command file.

parameters.name: refinement-name
parameters.desc: Name of the refinement placement

parameters.name: definition-name
parameters.desc: Name of the refinement definition

parameters.name: ref-eval-window
parameters.desc: Either the name of a defined refinement window (STRING), or a list of Ref/Eval windows, materials, and regions in the form (list "window" "refevalwin1" "material" "material1" "region" "region1" ...). If a list is specified, the refinement is performed at the intersection of all criteria

sdedr:define-refinement-region.description: Defines the placements information for a regionwise refinement.

parameters.name: placement-name
parameters.desc: Name of the placement

parameters.name: definition-name
parameters.desc: Name of the refinement definition

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Name of the region

sdedr:define-refinement-size.description: Creates a refinement size definition in the mesh command file.

parameters.name: definition-name
parameters.desc: Name of the refinement definition

parameters.name: max-x
parameters.desc: Maximum refinement size in x direction

parameters.name: max-y
parameters.desc: Optional; maximum refinement size in y direction

parameters.name: max-z
parameters.desc: Optional; maximum refinement size in z direction

parameters.name: min-x
parameters.desc: Minimum refinement size in x direction

parameters.name: min-y
parameters.desc: Optional; minimum refinement size in y direction

parameters.name: min-z
parameters.desc: Optional; minimum refinement size in z direction

sdedr:define-submesh.signature: (sdedr:define-submesh definition-name geofile-name file1 model file2 mode2 ... filen moden [(list field1 field2 ... fieldn)] | (sdedr:define-submesh definition-name geofile-name file1 model file2 mode2 ... filen moden [(list field1 conc1 field2 conc2 ... fieldn concn)])
sdedr:define-submesh.description: Creates a 1D external profile (submesh) in the mesh command file.

parameters.name: definition-name
parameters.desc: Name of the submesh definition

parameters.name: geofile-name
parameters.desc: Name of the geometry file

parameters.name: file[1..n]
parameters.desc: Data file names (paired with mode)

parameters.name: mode[1..n]
parameters.desc: File mode: "r" (read) | "i" (input) | "o" (output) | "w" (write) | "w=outfile-name" (write to specified file)

parameters.name: fields
parameters.desc: Optional list of field names, or field-concentration pairs

sdedr:define-submesh-placement.signature: (sdedr:define-submesh-placement placement-name definition-name evaluation-window ["PlacementType" eval-window-type] ["DecayLength" decay-length | "GaussDecayLength" gauss-decay-length] ["Replace" | "NoReplace" | "LocalReplace"] ["IgnoreMat"] ["MatchMaterialType"])
sdedr:define-submesh-placement.description: Creates a submesh placement in the mesh command file. To rotate, shift, or reflect submesh placements, use sdedr:transform-submesh-placement.

parameters.name: placement-name
parameters.desc: Name of the submesh placement

parameters.name: definition-name
parameters.desc: Name of the submesh definition

parameters.name: evaluation-window
parameters.desc: Name of a region, material, or Ref/Eval window, depending on eval-window-type

parameters.name: eval-window-type
parameters.desc: Optional; specifies the type of evaluation window. "evalwin" (default) for a Ref/Eval window, "material" for a material name, "region" for a region name

parameters.name: decay-length
parameters.desc: Optional decay length for blending (tagged "DecayLength")

parameters.name: options
parameters.desc: Optional: "Replace" replaces all other previously defined profiles; "NoReplace" adds the new profile to existing ones; "LocalReplace" replaces only the same species. Also supports "IgnoreMat" and "MatchMaterialType" options

sdedr:del-selected-drentity.description: Deletes a specified entity or list of entities.

parameters.name: entity
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of an entity to be deleted

parameters.name: entity-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of entity names to be deleted

sdedr:delete-multibox-placement.description: Deletes the specified multibox placement from the placements part of the command file.

parameters.name: placement-name
parameters.desc: Name of the multibox placement to delete

parameters.name: placement-name
parameters.desc: Name of the profile placement to delete

sdedr:delete-refeval-window.description: Deletes a Ref/Eval window or list of Ref/Eval windows. Can also accept the result of (sde:selected-refeval-windows).

parameters.name: bodies
parameters.desc: A single Ref/Eval window name (STRING) or a list of Ref/Eval window names (STRING LIST) to be deleted

sdedr:delete-refinement-placement.description: Deletes the specified refinement placement from the Placements section of the command file.

parameters.name: placement-name
parameters.desc: Name of the refinement placement to delete

parameters.name: placement-name
parameters.desc: Name of the submesh placement to delete

sdedr:get-cmdprecision.description: Returns the precision value of the mesh command file. The default value is 12.

sdedr:get-definition-list.description: Returns a list of definition names of one of the placement types.

parameters.name: placement-type
parameters.desc: Type of definition list to retrieve: "multibox" | "profile" | "refinement"

sdedr:get-placement-list.description: Returns a list of placement names of one of the placement types.

parameters.name: placement-type
parameters.desc: Type of placement list to retrieve: "multibox" | "profile" | "refinement"

parameters.name: mbox-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of multibox names (STRING LIST) to hide

sdedr:hide-profile.description: Hides the specified analytic doping profiles.

parameters.name: profile-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of doping profile names (STRING LIST) to hide

parameters.name: refinement-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of refinement names (STRING LIST) to hide

sdedr:hide-rewin.description: Hides the specified Ref/Eval window or windows.

parameters.name: rewin-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a Ref/Eval window name (STRING) or a list of Ref/Eval window names (STRING LIST) to hide

sdedr:offset-block.description: Generates a new offsetting block inside the Offsetting section of the mesh command file.

parameters.name: region
parameters.desc: Region name for the offsetting block (used with "region" keyword)

parameters.name: maxlevel
parameters.desc: Maximum refinement level for the offsetting block

sdedr:offset-global.description: Generates a new global offsetting parameters block. [信源不充分: T-2022 appendix does not include this function; description derived from N-2017 manual]

parameters.name: collisionDistance
parameters.desc: Collision distance parameter (tagged "collisionDistance")

parameters.name: factor
parameters.desc: Offsetting factor (tagged "factor")

parameters.name: hlocal
parameters.desc: Local mesh spacing (tagged "hlocal")

parameters.name: maxlevel
parameters.desc: Maximum refinement level (tagged "maxlevel")

parameters.name: minAngle
parameters.desc: Minimum angle parameter (tagged "minAngle")

sdedr:offset-interface.signature: (sdedr:offset-interface "region" region1 region2 "hlocal" hlocal "factor" factor "window" x1 y1 z1 x2 y2 z2) | (sdedr:offset-interface "material" material1 material2 "hlocal" hlocal "factor" factor "window" x1 y1 z1 x2 y2 z2)
sdedr:offset-interface.description: Generates a new offsetting interface block inside the Offsetting section of the mesh command file.

parameters.name: region1
parameters.desc: First region name (used with "region" keyword) or first material name (used with "material" keyword)

parameters.name: region2
parameters.desc: Second region name (used with "region" keyword) or second material name (used with "material" keyword)

parameters.name: hlocal
parameters.desc: Local mesh spacing at the interface

parameters.name: factor
parameters.desc: Offsetting factor

parameters.name: x1 y1 z1 x2 y2 z2
parameters.desc: Bounding window coordinates defining two opposite corners (six REAL values, tagged "window")

sdedr:read-cmd-file.description: Parses a specified mesh command file and initializes the doping-related and refinement-related data structures in Sentaurus Structure Editor. The command file entities can be visualized using the Placements Viewer and manipulated by using the sdedr Scheme extensions or the GUI menus.

parameters.name: cmd-file-name
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of a mesh command file to load

sdedr:redefine-refeval-window.description: Redefines a previously defined geometric Ref/Eval window. Used mainly in renaming existing Ref/Eval windows. Returns the ENTITY ID of the redefined ACIS body, or #f if the operation fails.

parameters.name: rfwin-name
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of the existing Ref/Eval window

parameters.name: new-rfwin-name
parameters.desc: Specifies the new name of the Ref/Eval window

parameters.name: rfwin-type
parameters.desc: Shape type: "Cuboid" | "Line" | "Point" | "Polygon" | "Rectangle". For "Cuboid", "Line", or "Rectangle", data is two opposite corner POSITIONs; for "Point", data is a single POSITION; for "Polygon", data is a POSITION LIST (first and last must be the same)

parameters.name: data
parameters.desc: POSITION or POSITION LIST depending on shape type

sdedr:refine-box.description: Defines a refinement window-style refinement box. Defines a refinement rectangle in 2D and a refinement cuboid in 3D.

parameters.name: refinement-name
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of a refinement box

parameters.name: pos_min
parameters.desc: Specifies the minimum coordinate of the box

parameters.name: pos_max
parameters.desc: Specifies the maximum coordinate of the box

parameters.name: spacing
parameters.desc: Specifies either a single value for mesh spacing in all directions, or a list of three values for spacing in each direction (x, y, z)

sdedr:refine-doping.signature: (sdedr:refine-doping spacing | sx sy sz ["MaxTransDiff" asinhdiff] ["material" material-name | "region" region-name])
sdedr:refine-doping.description: Adds a refinement based on doping (field:DopingConcentration) in an entire region or material.

parameters.name: spacing
parameters.desc: Specifies either a single value for mesh spacing in all directions, or three values (sx sy sz) for spacing in each direction

parameters.name: asinhdiff
parameters.desc: Optional; specifies the value for "MaxTransDiff". If not specified, 1.0 is used

parameters.name: target
parameters.desc: Optional; "material" followed by material name to restrict refinement to a specific material (defaults to "Silicon"), or "region" followed by region name

sdedr:refine-interface.signature: (sdedr:refine-interface "region" r1 r2 | "material" m1 m2 "hlocal" spacing ["factor" factor] ["window" x1 y1 z1 x2 y2 z2])
sdedr:refine-interface.description: Creates a refined mesh at an interface. The refinement is placed only in region r1 or material m1, unless a window is specified, in which case the placement is performed in the entire window.

parameters.name: type-keyword
parameters.desc: "region" for a region interface, or "material" for a material interface

parameters.name: name1
parameters.desc: First region or material name where refinement is applied

parameters.name: name2
parameters.desc: Second region or material name defining the interface

parameters.name: spacing
parameters.desc: Initial normal spacing at the interface (scalar)

parameters.name: factor
parameters.desc: Optional; determines how fast the spacing increases. The default is 2.0

parameters.name: window
parameters.desc: Optional; two opposite corners of a restriction window (x1 y1 z1 x2 y2 z2). The default is no window, with placement in r1 or m1

sdedr:set-cmdprecision.description: Sets the precision value of the mesh command file. Controls the number of digits written for Ref/Eval window coordinates. The vertex coordinates can be chopped and rounded. The default is 12.

parameters.name: cmdprecision
parameters.desc: Number of digits for coordinates in the mesh command file

sdedr:set-title.description: Sets the title of the command file. Information is written to the Title section. If not called explicitly, the title defaults to "Untitled".

parameters.name: cmd-file-title
parameters.desc: Specifies the title of the command file

parameters.name: mbox-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of multibox names (STRING LIST) to be displayed

sdedr:show-profile.description: Displays the specified analytic doping profiles.

parameters.name: profile-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of doping profile names (STRING LIST) to be displayed

parameters.name: refinement-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a list of refinement names (STRING LIST) to be displayed

sdedr:show-rewin.description: Displays the specified Ref/Eval window or windows.

parameters.name: rewin-list
parameters.desc: Specifies a Ref/Eval window name (STRING) or a list of Ref/Eval window names (STRING LIST) to be displayed

sdedr:transform-submesh-placement.description: Defines transform operations applied to the named external profile placement. Operations are appended to any existing series and performed in order. Use sdedr:clear-submesh-placement-transform to clear the list.

parameters.name: placement-name
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of an external profile placement

parameters.name: transform
parameters.desc: Transform operations: "Reflect" axis ("X" | "Y" | "Z"), "Rotation" angle axis, or "ShiftVector" (gvector x y z). Any number of operations can be specified; they are performed in the order listed

sdedr:write-cmd-file.description: Writes all doping-related and refinement-related data to the specified mesh command file. The command file can be used during a subsequent meshing action together with the generated boundary file.

parameters.name: cmd-file-name
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of a mesh command file to write

sdedr:write-scaled-cmd-file.description: Writes all doping-related and refinement-related data to the specified mesh command file after scaling some dimensions. All position and length parameters are multiplied by the scaling factor; all gradient parameters are divided by it. Note: Ref/Eval windows must be scaled separately using sdegeo:scale before calling this function.

parameters.name: cmd-file-name
parameters.desc: Specifies the name of a mesh command file to write

parameters.name: scaling-factor
parameters.desc: Scaling factor; all position and length parameters are multiplied by this factor, all gradient parameters are divided by it

sdeepi:create-layerstack.description: Creates a planar layer stack structure from the CSV input data.

parameters.name: file-name
parameters.desc: STRING path to the CSV input data file containing the planar layer stack definition

sdeepi:publish-global-vars.description: Defines the epi layer stack variables as global Scheme variables.

parameters.name: make-global-vars
parameters.desc: BOOLEAN: if #t, all published global and layer variables are retained as global Scheme variables after stack creation; if #f (default), no variables are retained to avoid overwriting existing Scheme variables

sdeepi:scm.description: Generates a Scheme script for creating a planar layer stack structure from CSV input data.

parameters.name: file-name
parameters.desc: STRING path to the CSV input data file

parameters.name: output-file-name
parameters.desc: Optional STRING path for the generated Scheme script; defaults to file-name with .scm extension

sdeepi:tcl.description: Generates a Tcl script containing all layer stack data from the CSV input file as Tcl variables.

parameters.name: file-name
parameters.desc: STRING path to the CSV input data file

parameters.name: output-file-name
parameters.desc: Optional STRING path for the generated Tcl script; defaults to file-name with .tcl extension

sdeicwb:clear.description: Removes all IC WorkBench (ICWB) related data from memory.

sdeicwb:contact.signature: (sdeicwb:contact ("label.name" label-name | "layer.name" layer-name) ("type" "box" "material" material ["adjacent.material" adjacent-material] (["boxheight" boxheight] | ["hi" <n> "lo" <n>]) | "type" "point" "material" material ["height" <n>] ["replace" #t | #f]) ["name" name])
sdeicwb:contact.description: Creates contacts for subsequent device simulations tied to a mask or a text label in the TCAD layout file.

parameters.name: label-name
parameters.desc: Name of the text label to use for contact placement; the anchor point location (or locations) of the text label is used for lateral placement

parameters.name: layer-name
parameters.desc: Name of the layer to use for contact placement; must be created beforehand (e.g., with sdeicwb:generate-mask-by-layer-name or sdepe:generate-mask)

parameters.name: type
parameters.desc: "box" or "point" — the type of contact to create

parameters.name: material
parameters.desc: Material name for the contact region; the contact area is defined by the surface of this material inside the box (box-type) or the boundary elements of this region (point-type)

parameters.name: adjacent-material
parameters.desc: Optional; restrict contact area to only the part of the surface that also touches this material (box-type only)

parameters.name: boxheight
parameters.desc: Optional; controls the vertical extent of the box-type contact; if not given, the vertical extent is determined automatically based on the topmost interface with the specified material

parameters.name: hi
parameters.desc: Optional; upper vertical coordinate of box-type contact; used with lo as an alternative to boxheight

parameters.name: lo
parameters.desc: Optional; lower vertical coordinate of box-type contact; if not explicitly given, the vertical extent is determined automatically based on the topmost interface with the specified material

parameters.name: height
parameters.desc: Optional; vertical position of point-type contact; if not given, determined automatically based on interfaces with the specified material

parameters.name: replace
parameters.desc: Optional; if #t, remove the original region of the point-type contact (point-type only)

parameters.name: name
parameters.desc: Optional; custom name for the contact, defaults to the name of the layer or text label specified

sdeicwb:create-boxes-from-layer.description: Creates cuboids with lateral extent given by polygon bounding boxes from the specified ICWB layer.

parameters.name: lname
parameters.desc: Name of a layer in the ICWB TCAD layout; the polygon bounding boxes from this layer define the lateral extent

parameters.name: top
parameters.desc: Top coordinate of the box (vertical extent)

parameters.name: bot
parameters.desc: Bottom coordinate of the box (vertical extent)

parameters.name: region-material
parameters.desc: Material for the region to create inside the cuboids

parameters.name: region-name
parameters.desc: Name for the region to create inside the cuboids

sdeicwb:define-refinement-from-layer.signature: (sdeicwb:define-refinement-from-layer "lname" lname ["rname" rname] ["oversize" oversize] "top" top "bot" bot "dlrmin" dlrmin "dlrmax" dlrmax "dbtmin" dbtmin "dbtmax" dbtmax ["dbfmin" dbfmin] ["dbfmax" dbfmax] ["material" material] ["region" region] ["use-bbox" use-bbox])
sdeicwb:define-refinement-from-layer.description: Creates refinement windows with lateral extent from ICWB layers and vertical extent from arguments; also generates 3D refinement size settings.

parameters.name: lname
parameters.desc: Name of a layer; for 3D domains, a refinement window is created for each polygon found in the specified layer

parameters.name: rname
parameters.desc: Optional; name of refinement window, defaults to the layer name; the resulting refinement window has the form RPlace.<name>___<counter>

parameters.name: oversize
parameters.desc: Optional; nonzero value subtracted from or added to the minimum and maximum polygon bounding box coordinates to extend the refinement area beyond the actual polygon extent

parameters.name: top
parameters.desc: Top coordinate of the refinement window (vertical extent)

parameters.name: bot
parameters.desc: Bottom coordinate of the refinement window (vertical extent)

parameters.name: dlrmin
parameters.desc: Minimum refinement size in the left-right dimension

parameters.name: dlrmax
parameters.desc: Maximum refinement size in the left-right dimension

parameters.name: dbtmin
parameters.desc: Minimum refinement size in the bottom-top dimension

parameters.name: dbtmax
parameters.desc: Maximum refinement size in the bottom-top dimension

parameters.name: dbfmin
parameters.desc: Optional; minimum refinement size in the back-front dimension (3D only)

parameters.name: dbfmax
parameters.desc: Optional; maximum refinement size in the back-front dimension (3D only)

parameters.name: material
parameters.desc: Optional; DATEX material name or list of DATEX material names to restrict refinement to the specified bodies within the refinement box

parameters.name: region
parameters.desc: Optional; region name or list of region names to restrict refinement to the specified bodies within the refinement box

parameters.name: use-bbox
parameters.desc: Optional; if #t (default), use the bounding box of the polygon as a cuboid volume; if #f, use the polygon shape itself to create a 3D prism (2D polygons in 3D structures only); when #f, oversize is ignored

sdeicwb:down.description: Returns a coordinate adjusted downward, adapting to the current coordinate system convention.

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: The base coordinate value (REAL)

parameters.name: increment
parameters.desc: The increment to apply; for UCS returns value + increment, for DF-ISE returns value - increment (REAL)

sdeicwb:gds2mac.signature: (sdeicwb:gds2mac "gds.file" gdsfile "cell" cellname "layer.names" lnames "layer.numbers" lnumbers "sim3d" sim3ddomain "scale" scale "domain.name" dname "mac.file" macfile ["recenter" #t | #f] ["stretches" stretches] ["loadmacfile" #t | #f])
sdeicwb:gds2mac.description: Converts a GDS layout file to a .mac file and optionally loads it for further processing by sdeicwb commands.

parameters.name: gdsfile
parameters.desc: Name of the GDS file to convert

parameters.name: cellname
parameters.desc: Name of the GDS cell; use "?" as a wildcard to use the active (open) GDS cell

parameters.name: lnames
parameters.desc: String list of layer names corresponding to the GDS layers

parameters.name: lnumbers
parameters.desc: String list of corresponding layer numbers (e.g., "1:0", "2:0"); can be obtained from sdeicwb:mapreader

parameters.name: sim3ddomain
parameters.desc: Real list of four numbers defining the layout area to convert in (unscaled) layout coordinates

parameters.name: scale
parameters.desc: Mask scaling factor applied to convert layout units

parameters.name: dname
parameters.desc: Domain name for the converted layout

parameters.name: macfile
parameters.desc: Name of the output .mac file to generate

parameters.name: recenter
parameters.desc: Optional; if #t, center the loaded mask (default #f); translation to the (0,0) origin is switched off when set to #f

parameters.name: stretches
parameters.desc: Optional; list of stretches with each defined by a segment with two points; the segment must cross the bounding box of the 3D domain

parameters.name: loadmacfile
parameters.desc: Optional; if #f, suppress automatic loading of the converted .mac file (default #t)

sdeicwb:generate-mask-by-layer-name.description: Creates a mask based on given ICWB layer names referencing a previously loaded macro file and selected domain.

parameters.name: maskname
parameters.desc: Name for the generated mask (STRING)

parameters.name: layernames
parameters.desc: A single layer name (STRING) or a list of layer names (STRING LIST) from the previously loaded ICWB macro file

parameters.name: with-overlaps
parameters.desc: Optional; if #t, resolve overlapping mask polygons by uniting the overlapping parts (default #f)

parameters.name: do-entity-check
parameters.desc: Optional; if #t, perform an entity check and boundary repair to resolve problems found by the entity checker (default #f)

sdeicwb:get-back.description: Returns the back bounding-box coordinate of the current domains with stretches already applied.

sdeicwb:get-dimension.description: Returns the dimension of the current domain as an integer (1=Point, 2=Gauge, 3=Highlight).

sdeicwb:get-domains.description: Returns a list of current domain names set by sdeicwb:set-domain.

sdeicwb:get-front.description: Returns the front bounding-box coordinate of the current domains with stretches already applied.

sdeicwb:get-global-bot.description: Returns the bottom coordinate of the global bounding box in a coordinate system-independent manner.

sdeicwb:get-global-top.description: Returns the top coordinate of the global bounding box in a coordinate system-independent manner.

sdeicwb:get-label.description: Returns a list containing information about the specified label (layer name, text label, and coordinates).

parameters.name: label-name
parameters.desc: The label name to query (STRING); returns the layer name, text label (both strings), and label text coordinates (two real numbers)

sdeicwb:get-label-for-layer.description: Returns a list containing label information (layer name, text label, and coordinates) for the specified layer.

parameters.name: layer-name
parameters.desc: The layer name to query (STRING); returns the layer name, text label (both strings), and label text coordinates (two real numbers)

sdeicwb:get-labels.description: Returns a list of all label information lists, each containing layer name, text label, and coordinates.

sdeicwb:get-layer-ids.description: Returns a list of layer IDs from the ICWB mask file previously loaded by sdeicwb:load-file.

sdeicwb:get-layer-names.description: Returns a list of layer names from the ICWB mask file previously loaded by sdeicwb:load-file.

sdeicwb:get-layer-polygon-midpoints.description: Computes the midpoints of the segments or polygons in a layer, used to locate objects created by layout-driven operations.

parameters.name: layer-name
parameters.desc: The name of the layer (STRING)

parameters.name: height
parameters.desc: The height value as the third coordinate (REAL); used to construct 3D positions from the 2D polygon midpoints

sdeicwb:get-left.description: Returns the left bounding-box coordinate of the current domains in SDE coordinates with stretches already applied.

sdeicwb:get-polygon-bounding-boxes-by-la.description: Returns the bounding boxes of polygons on a given layer and the current domain as diagonal segments.

parameters.name: layer-name
parameters.desc: The name of the layer (STRING); requires the current domain to be set first with sdeicwb:set-domain

sdeicwb:get-polygon-by-name.description: Returns polygon coordinates for a given polygon name.

parameters.name: polygon-name
parameters.desc: The name of the polygon (STRING); polygons are named as they are read in using sdeicwb:load-file

sdeicwb:get-polygon-names-by-layer-name.description: Returns the names of polygons on the given layer.

parameters.name: layer-name
parameters.desc: The name of the layer (STRING); returns a list of polygon names on that layer

sdeicwb:get-region-bot.description: Returns the bottom coordinate of a given region using a coordinate-system-independent syntax.

parameters.name: body
parameters.desc: The body ID of the region (BODY); for UCS returns the maximum x-coordinate, for DF-ISE returns the minimum z-coordinate of the region bounding box

sdeicwb:get-region-top.description: Returns the top coordinate of a given region using a coordinate-system-independent syntax.

parameters.name: body
parameters.desc: The body ID of the region (BODY); for UCS returns the minimum x-coordinate, for DF-ISE returns the maximum z-coordinate of the region bounding box

sdeicwb:get-right.description: Returns the right bounding-box coordinate of the current domains in SDE coordinates with stretches already applied.

sdeicwb:get-xmax.description: Returns the xmax bounding-box coordinate of the current domains in ICWB coordinates with stretches already applied.

sdeicwb:get-xmin.description: Returns the xmin bounding-box coordinate of the current domains in ICWB coordinates with stretches already applied.

sdeicwb:get-ymax.description: Returns the ymax bounding-box coordinate of the current domains in ICWB coordinates with stretches already applied.

sdeicwb:get-ymin.description: Returns the ymin bounding-box coordinate of the current domains in ICWB coordinates with stretches already applied.

sdeicwb:load-file.description: Loads an ICWB macro file; all ICWB coordinates are optionally scaled and/or centered.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.desc: Path to the ICWB macro file; prerequisite to other sdeicwb commands that act on domains and masks in that file

parameters.name: scale
parameters.desc: Optional scaling factor (REAL); all ICWB coordinates are multiplied by this value as they are read

parameters.name: center?
parameters.desc: Optional flag; if #t, center the loaded mask by modifying vertex coordinates (vertex coordinate minus highlight coordinate)

sdeicwb:mapreader.description: Parses a .map file and returns the layer names and the layer numbers as two lists.

parameters.name: fname
parameters.desc: Path to the .map file (STRING); the file must have a two-column format: <layername><space or tab><layernumber>

sdeicwb:set-domain.description: Sets the current domain to the given domain name or list of domain names.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.desc: A domain name (STRING) or a list of domain names (STRING LIST); prerequisite for other sdeicwb commands that depend on the current domain

sdeicwb:stretch.description: Applies the given stretch by the given amount to the current domains.

parameters.name: stretch-name
parameters.desc: The name of the stretch to apply (STRING)

parameters.name: stretch-amount
parameters.desc: The amount by which to stretch (REAL); the order of applied stretches is important as the location of other stretches can change

sdeicwb:up.description: Increases or decreases vertical positions depending on the coordinate system convention (UCS: value - increment; DF-ISE: value + increment).

parameters.name: value
parameters.desc: The base coordinate value (REAL)

parameters.name: increment
parameters.desc: The increment to apply; for UCS returns value - increment, for DF-ISE returns value + increment (REAL)

sdeio:assign-local-refs.description: Automatically assigns local refinement to optimize the quality of the tessellated boundary output.

parameters.name: lref
parameters.desc: Refinement level between 0 and 1 (REAL). Values closer to 0 mean less refinement, closer to 1 mean more. A reasonable range is 0.2-0.8. The refinement for each face is determined solely by local face geometry (curvature and other properties)

sdeio:find-refinement-tolerances.description: Checks the model geometry and finds appropriate surface and normal tolerances to prevent topological problems in tessellated boundary output.

sdeio:read-dfise-mask.description: Loads a mask layout file (.1yt) into the modeler for use in process emulation pattern operations.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.desc: Name of the mask layout file with .1yt extension (STRING). May include a path. Mask polarities are not fixed; each mask can be used as either 'light' or 'dark' field

sdeio:read-tdr.description: Extracts the boundary from a TDR mesh file and loads it into Sentaurus Structure Editor, optionally skipping doping information.

parameters.name: file-name
parameters.desc: Path to the TDR mesh file (STRING). If the file already contains boundary geometry (typical of TDR files from Sentaurus Process and Sentaurus Interconnect), it is loaded directly

parameters.name: "skip-doping"
parameters.desc: Optional literal string to skip creating submesh definition and placement from doping information contained in the TDR file

sdeio:read-tdr-bnd.description: Loads a TDR boundary file into Sentaurus Structure Editor, converting TDR regions to ACIS bodies with material properties and contact attributes.

parameters.name: file-name
parameters.desc: Path to the TDR boundary file (STRING). Each TDR region element is converted to an ACIS body; 2D regions become nonuniform sheet bodies, 3D regions become manifold solids. Contact attributes are attached to edges (2D) or faces (3D)

sdeio:save-1d-tdr-bnd.description: Creates a 1D TDR boundary output file extracted from a simple 2D geometry along the up coordinate axis.

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: List of bodies or "all" (BODY LIST | STRING). The 1D geometry is extracted from these entities along the up direction defined by sde:set-process-up-direction

parameters.name: file-name
parameters.desc: Output TDR boundary file name (STRING)

parameters.name: options
parameters.desc: Optional tessellation controls: "aspect ratio" (REAL, default 0), "aut-tolerance-control" (BOOLEAN, default #t), "faceter" ("v1"|"v2", default "v1"), "max edge length" (REAL, default 0), "normal tolerance" (REAL, default 15), "surface tolerance" (REAL, default 0), "vertex-precision" (INTEGER 8-16, default bndprecision), writeinterface (BOOLEAN, optional)

sdeio:save-2d-tdr-bnd.description: Generates a 2D TDR boundary output file from the specified bodies.

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: List of bodies or "all" (BODY LIST | STRING)

parameters.name: file-name
parameters.desc: Output TDR boundary file name (STRING)

parameters.name: options
parameters.desc: Optional tessellation controls: writeinterface (BOOLEAN), saveprecision (INTEGER), "aut-tolerance-control" (BOOLEAN, default #t), "vertex-precision" (INTEGER 8-16, default bndprecision), "faceter" ("v1"|"v2", default "v1"), "surface tolerance" (REAL, default 0), "normal tolerance" (REAL, default 15), "aspect ratio" (REAL, default 0), "max edge length" (REAL, default 0). Keyword order is arbitrary after the first two required arguments

sdeio:save-3d-tdr-bnd.description: Generates a 3D TDR boundary output file from the specified bodies.

parameters.name: body-list
parameters.desc: List of bodies or "all" (BODY LIST | STRING)

parameters.name: file-name
parameters.desc: Output TDR boundary file name (STRING)
